translations.text: And the good land - its vegetation emerges by permission of its Lord; but that which is bad - nothing emerges except sparsely, with difficulty. Thus do We diversify the signs for a people who are grateful.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: A good land produces plants, by the permission of its Lord, but a wicked land produces only miserable, bitter plants. Thus do We show a variety of evidence for those who give thanks.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And as for the good land, its vegetation springs forth (abundantly) by the permission of its Lord, and (as for) that which is inferior (its herbage) comes forth but scantily; thus do We repeat the communications for a people who give thanks.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Vegetation comes out of good land in abundance by the will of its Lord, but out of bad land only scantily. Thus We explain Our signs in diverse ways for those who give thanks.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: From the land that is clean and good, by the will of its Cherisher, springs up produce, (rich) after its kind: but from the land that is bad, springs up nothing but that which is niggardly: thus do we explain the signs by various (symbols) to those who are grateful.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waalbaladu alttayyibu yakhruju nabatuhu biithni rabbihi waallathee khabutha la yakhruju illa nakidan kathalika nusarrifu alayati liqawmin yashkuroona

59.arabic: لَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا نُوحًا إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِۦ فَقَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرُهُۥٓ إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ عَلَيْكُمْ عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: We sent Noah to his people, and he said: "O people worship God; you have no other god but He; for I fear the retribution of the great Day may fall on you."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Indeed We sent Nooh to his people – he therefore said, “O my people! Worship Allah – you do not have any God except Him; indeed I fear for you the punishment of the Great Day (of Resurrection).”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And We sent Noah to his people; and he said, 'O my people, serve God! You have no god other' than He; truly, I fear for you the chastisement of a dreadful day.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Assuredly We sent Nuh unto his people, and he said: O my people! worship Allah; no god ye have but He; verily I fear for you the torment of a mighty day.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Indeed, We sent Nuh (Noah) to his people and he said: "O my people! Worship Allah! You have no other Ilah (God) but Him. (La ilaha ill-Allah: none has the right to be worshipped but Allah). Certainly, I fear for you the torment of a Great Day!"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: We sent Noah to his people. He said, “O my people! Worship God; you have no god other than Him. I fear for you the punishment of a tremendous Day.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Indeed We sent forth Noah to his people and he said: 'O my people! Serve Allah, you have no other god than Him Indeed I fear for you the chastisement of an awesome Day.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Indeed, We sent Nuh to his people and he said: "O my people! Worship Allah! You have no other god but Him. Certainly, I fear for you the torment of a Great Day!"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: We sent Noah (of old) unto his people, and he said: O my people! Serve Allah. Ye have no other Allah save Him. Lo! I fear for you the retribution of an Awful Day.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Certainly We sent Noah to his people. He said, ‘O my people, worship Allah! You have no other god besides Him. Indeed I fear for you the punishment of a tremendous day.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We sent forth Noah to his nation. He said: 'Worship Allah, my people for you have no god except He. I fear for you the punishment of a great Day.'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: We had certainly sent Noah to his people, and he said, "O my people, worship Allah; you have no deity other than Him. Indeed, I fear for you the punishment of a tremendous Day.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We sent Noah to his people. He told them, "Worship God for He is your only Lord. I am afraid of the punishment that you might suffer on the great Day (of Judgment)".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Certainly We sent Nuh to his people, so he said: O my people! serve Allah, you have no god other than Him; surely I fear for you the chastisement of a grievous day.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We sent Noah to his people. He said, "O my people, worship God; you have no other god but He. I fear for you the punishment of a dreadful Day,"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: We sent Noah to his people. He said: "O my people! worship Allah! ye have no other god but Him. I fear for you the punishment of a dreadful day!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Laqad arsalna noohan ila qawmihi faqala ya qawmi oAAbudoo Allaha ma lakum min ilahin ghayruhu innee akhafu AAalaykum AAathaba yawmin AAatheemin

60.arabic: قَالَ ٱلْمَلَأُ مِن قَوْمِهِۦٓ إِنَّا لَنَرَىٰكَ فِى ضَلَـٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The elders of his people replied: "We see clearly that you have gone astray."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: The leaders of his people said, “Indeed we see you in open error.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Said the Council of his people, 'We see thee in manifest error.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: The chiefs of his people said: verily we see thee in error manifest.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: The leaders of his people said: "Verily, we see you in plain error."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: The dignitaries among his people said, “We see that you are in obvious error.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: The leading men of his people replied: 'We see that you are in palpable error.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: The leaders of his people said: "Verily, we see you in plain error."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: The chieftains of his people said: Lo! we see thee surely in plain error.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: The elite of his people said, ‘Indeed we see you in manifest error.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: But the assembly of his nation said: 'We can see that you are in clear error.'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Said the eminent among his people, "Indeed, we see you in clear error."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: A group of his people said to him, "You are absolutely wrong."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: The chiefs of his people said: Most surely we see you in clear error.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: but the leading men of his people said, "Truly, we see that you are obviously lost in error!"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: The leaders of his people said: "Ah! we see thee evidently wandering (in mind)."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qala almalao min qawmihi inna lanaraka fee dalalin mubeenin

61.arabic: قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ لَيْسَ بِى ضَلَـٰلَةٌ وَلَـٰكِنِّى رَسُولٌ مِّن رَّبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: "I have not gone astray, O my people," he said, "but have been sent by my Lord, the creator of all the worlds.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: He said, “O my people! There is no straying in me – I am in fact a Noble Messenger from the Lord Of The Creation.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Said he, 'My people, there is no error in me; but I am a Messenger from the Lord of all Being.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: He said: O my people! not with me is the error, but I am an apostle from the Lord of the worlds.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: [Nuh (Noah)] said: "O my people! There is no error in me, but I am a Messenger from the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists)!

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He said, “O my people, I am not in error, but I am a messenger from the Lord of the Worlds.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: He said: 'O my people! There is no error in me, but I am a Messenger from the Lord of the universe.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: [Nuh] said: "O my people! There is no error in me, but I am a Messenger from the Lord of all that exists!

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He said: O my people! There is no error in me, but I am a messenger from the Lord of the Worlds.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He said, ‘O my people, I am not in error. Rather, I am an apostle from the Lord of all the worlds.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: 'I am not in error, my nation' he replied. 'I am a Messenger from the Lord of the Worlds

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [Noah] said, "O my people, there is not error in me, but I am a messenger from the Lord of the worlds."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Noah) said, "My people, I am not in any error, rather I am a Messenger from the Lord of the Universe,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He said: O my people! there is no error in me, but I am an apostle from the Lord of the worlds.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Said [Noah], "O my people! I am not in error. Indeed, I am a messenger from the Lord of the Worlds,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: He said: "O my people! No wandering is there in my (mind): on the contrary I am a messenger from the Lord and Cherisher of the worlds!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qala ya qawmi laysa bee dalalatun walakinnee rasoolun min rabbi alAAalameena

62.arabic: أُبَلِّغُكُمْ رِسَـٰلَـٰتِ رَبِّى وَأَنصَحُ لَكُمْ وَأَعْلَمُ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: I bring to you the messages of my Lord, and give you sincere advice, for I know from God what you do not know.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “Conveying to you the messages of my Lord and wishing good for you, and I know from Allah what you do not know.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: I deliver to you the Messages of my Lord, and I advise you sincerely; for I know from God that you know not.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: I preach unto you the messages of my Lord and I councel you good, and I know from Allah which ye know not.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "I convey unto you the Messages of my Lord and give sincere advice to you. And I know from Allah what you know not.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “I deliver to you the messages of my Lord, and I advise you, and I know from God what you do not know.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: I convey to you the messages of my Lord, give you sincere advice, and I know from Allah that which you do not know.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: I convey unto you the Messages of my Lord and give sincere advice to you. And I know from Allah what you know not."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: I convey unto you the messages of my Lord and give good counsel unto you, and know from Allah that which ye know not.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: I communicate to you the messages of my Lord, and I am your well-wisher, and I know from Allah what you do not know.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: (sent) to convey to you the messages of my Lord and to advise you, for I know from Allah what you do not know.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: I convey to you the messages of my Lord and advise you; and I know from Allah what you do not know.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: sent to preach to you the message of my Lord and to give you good advice. I know what you do not know about God.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: I deliver to you the messages of my Lord, and I offer you good advice and I know from Allah what you do not know.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: I am conveying my Lord's messages to you and giving you sincere advice. I know things from God that you do not.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "I but fulfil towards you the duties of my Lord's mission: Sincere is my advice to you, and I know from Allah something that ye know not.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Oballighukum risalati rabbee waansahu lakum waaAAlamu mina Allahi ma la taAAlamoona

63.arabic: أَوَعَجِبْتُمْ أَن جَآءَكُمْ ذِكْرٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ عَلَىٰ رَجُلٍ مِّنكُمْ لِيُنذِرَكُمْ وَلِتَتَّقُوا۟ وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Do you wonder that a warning has come to you from your Lord through a man who is one of you, and warns you to take heed for yourselves and fear God? You might be treated with mercy."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “And are you surprised that an advice came to you from your Lord through a man amongst you, so that he may warn you and that you may fear, and so that there be mercy upon you?”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: What, do you wonder that a reminder from your Lord should come to you by the lips of a man from among you? That he may warn you, and you be godfearing, haply to find mercy.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Marvel ye that an admonition from your Lord should come unto you upon a man from amongst yourselves, in order that the may warn you and that ye may fear, and that haply ye may be shewn mercy?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "Do you wonder that there has come to you a Reminder from your Lord through a man from amongst you, that he may warn you, so that you may fear Allah and that you may receive (His) Mercy?"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “Do you wonder that a reminder has come to you from your Lord, through a man from among you, to warn you, and to lead you to righteousness, so that you may attain mercy?”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Do you wonder that admonition should come to you from your Lord through a man from amongst yourselves that he may warn you, that you may avoid evil and that mercy may be shown to you?'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "Do you wonder that there has come to you a Reminder from your Lord through a man from among you, that he may warn you, so that you may fear Allah and that you may receive (His) mercy"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Marvel ye that there should come unto you a Reminder from your Lord by means of a man among you, that he may warn you, and that ye may keep from evil, and that haply ye may find mercy.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Do you consider it odd that a reminder from your Lord should come to you through a man from among yourselves, to warn you so that you may be Godwary and so that you may receive His mercy?’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Do you wonder that a Remembrance should come to you from your Lord to a man from yourselves in order that he may warn you and that you may be cautious so that mercy comes upon you'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Then do you wonder that there has come to you a reminder from your Lord through a man from among you, that he may warn you and that you may fear Allah so you might receive mercy."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Does it seem strange to you that a reminder from your Lord should be sent to a man, from among you, to warn you so that you might receive mercy?"

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: What! do you wonder that a reminder has come to you from your Lord through a man from among you, that he might warn you and that you might guard (against evil) and so that mercy may be shown to you?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Do you find it so strange that a message should come from your Lord through a man from among yourselves, so that he may warn you and so that you may fear God and be shown mercy?"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "Do ye wonder that there hath come to you a message from your Lord, through a man of your own people, to warn you,- so that ye may fear Allah and haply receive His Mercy?"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: AwaAAajibtum an jaakum thikrun min rabbikum AAala rajulin minkum liyunthirakum walitattaqoo walaAAallakum turhamoona

64.arabic: فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَأَنجَيْنَـٰهُ وَٱلَّذِينَ مَعَهُۥ فِى ٱلْفُلْكِ وَأَغْرَقْنَا ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَآ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ قَوْمًا عَمِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But they called him a liar, and We saved him and those with him in the Ark, and drowned the others who rejected Our signs, for they were a people purblind.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: In response they denied him, so We rescued him and those with him in the ship, and We drowned those who denied Our signs; indeed they were a blind group.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: But they cried him lies; so We delivered him, and those with him, in the Ark, and We drowned those who cried lies to Our signs; assuredly they were a blind people.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Then they belied him; there upon We delivered him and those with him in the ark, and drowned those who belied Our signs; verily they were a people blind.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: But they belied him, so We saved him and those along with him in the ship, and We drowned those who belied Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.). They were indeed a blind people.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: But they called him a liar. So We saved him and those with him in the Ark, and We drowned those who rejected Our revelations. They were blind people.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: But they charged him with falsehood. Thereupon We delivered Noah and those who were with him in the Ark, and caused those who rejected Our signs as false to be drowned. Surely they were a blind folk.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: But they belied him, so We saved him and those along with him in the Fulk, and We drowned those who belied Our Ayat. They were indeed a blind people.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: But they denied him, so We saved him and those with him in the ship, and We drowned those who denied Our tokens. Lo! they were blind folk.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: But they denied him. So We delivered him and those who were with him in the ark, and We drowned those who denied Our signs. Indeed, they were a blind lot.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: (But) they belied him, so We saved him and those who were with him in the Ark, and We drowned those who belied Our verses. Surely, they were a blind nation.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: But they denied him, so We saved him and those who were with him in the ship. And We drowned those who denied Our signs. Indeed, they were a blind people.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They accused him of telling lies. So We saved him and his companions in an ark and drowned those who called Our revelations mere lies. They were, no doubt, a blind people.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: But they called him a liar, so We delivered him and those with him in the ark, and We drowned those who rejected Our communications; surely they were a blind people.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: But they denied him, so We saved him and those with him in the Ark, and We drowned those who rejected Our signs. They were indeed a blind people.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: But they rejected him, and We delivered him, and those with him, in the Ark: but We overwhelmed in the flood those who rejected Our signs. They were indeed a blind people!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fakaththaboohu faanjaynahu waallatheena maAAahu fee alfulki waaghraqna allatheena kaththaboo biayatina innahum kanoo qawman AAameena

65.arabic: وَإِلَىٰ عَادٍ أَخَاهُمْ هُودًا ۗ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرُهُۥٓ ۚ أَفَلَا تَتَّقُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And We sent Hud, their brother, to the people of 'Ad. He said: "O you people, worship God, for you have no other god but He. Will you not take heed for yourselves?"

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And We sent Hud to the people of Aad from their own community; he said, “O my people! Worship Allah – you do not have any God except Him; so do you not fear?”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And to Ad their brother Hood; he said, 'O my people, serve God! You have no god other than He; will you not be godfearing?'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And unto 'Ad We sent their brothers Hud. He said: O my people! worship Allah, no god ye have but He; Fear ye not?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And to 'Ad (people, We sent) their brother Hud. He said: "O my people! Worship Allah! You have no other Ilah (God) but Him. (La ilaha ill-Allah: none has the right to be worshipped but Allah). Will you not fear (Allah)?"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And to Aad, their brother Hud. He said, “O my people! Worship God; you have no god other than Him. Will you not take heed?”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And to 'Ad We sent forth their brother Hud. He said: 'O my people! Serve Allah; you have no other god than Him. Will you, then, not avoid evil?'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And to `Ad (the people, We sent) their brother Hud. He said: "O my people! Worship Allah! You have no other god but Him. Will you then not have Taqwa"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And unto (the tribe of) A'ad (We sent) their brother, Hud. He said: O my people! Serve Allah. Ye have no other Allah save Him. Will ye not ward off (evil)?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And to [the people of] ‘Ad [We sent] Hud, their brother. He said, ‘O my people, worship Allah! You have no other god besides Him. Will you not then be wary [of Him]?’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And to (the people of) Aad We sent their brother, Hood. He said: 'Worship Allah, my nation, for you have no god except He. Will you not then be cautious'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And to the 'Aad [We sent] their brother Hud. He said, "O my people, worship Allah; you have no deity other than Him. Then will you not fear Him?"

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We sent Hud to his brethren, the tribe of `Ad, who told them, "Worship God for He is your only Lord. Will you not become pious?"

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And to Ad (We sent) their brother Hud. He said: O my people! serve Allah, you have no god other than Him; will you not then guard (against evil)?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: To the people of 'Ad We sent their brother, Hud. He said, "O my people, worship God, you have no other god but He. Then will you not be God-fearing?"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: To the 'Ad people, (We sent) Hud, one of their (own) brethren: He said: O my people! worship Allah! ye have no other god but Him will ye not fear (Allah)?"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waila AAadin akhahum hoodan qala ya qawmi oAAbudoo Allaha ma lakum min ilahin ghayruhu afala tattaqoona

66.arabic: قَالَ ٱلْمَلَأُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِن قَوْمِهِۦٓ إِنَّا لَنَرَىٰكَ فِى سَفَاهَةٍ وَإِنَّا لَنَظُنُّكَ مِنَ ٱلْكَـٰذِبِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The chiefs of his people who were infidels replied: "We find you full of folly, and a liar to boot."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: The disbelieving leaders of his people said, “Indeed we consider you foolish and think you are a liar.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Said the Council of the unbelievers of his people, 'We see thee in folly, and we think that thou art one of the liars.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: The chiefs of those who disbelived among his people said: verily we see thee in folly, and verily we deem thee to be of the liars.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: The leaders of those who disbelieved among his people said: "Verily, we see you in foolishness, and verily, we think you are one of the liars."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: The elite of his people who disbelieved said, “We see foolishness in you, and we think that you are a liar.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: The unbelievers among the leading men of his people said: 'Indeed we see you in folly, and consider you to be liars.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: The leaders of those who disbelieved among his people said: "Verily, we see you in foolishness, and verily, we think you are one of the liars."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: The chieftains of his people, who were disbelieving, said: Lo! we surely see thee in foolishness, and lo! we deem thee of the liars.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: The elite of his people who were faithless said, ‘Indeed we see you to be in folly, and indeed we consider you to be a liar.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: The disbelieving assembly of his nation said: 'We see you in foolishness, and think that you are of the liars'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Said the eminent ones who disbelieved among his people, "Indeed, we see you in foolishness, and indeed, we think you are of the liars."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: A group of the unbelievers among his people said, "You look to us like a fool and we think that you are a liar".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: The chiefs of those who disbelieved from among his people said: Most surely we see you in folly, and most surely we think you to be of the liars.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: The leading men of his people who refused to acknowledge the truth, said, "We can see you are a foolish man, and we think you are lying."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: The leaders of the Unbelievers among his people said: "Ah! we see thou art an imbecile!" and "We think thou art a liar!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qala almalao allatheena kafaroo min qawmihi inna lanaraka fee safahatin wainna lanathunnuka mina alkathibeena

67.arabic: قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ لَيْسَ بِى سَفَاهَةٌ وَلَـٰكِنِّى رَسُولٌ مِّن رَّبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: "I am not a fool, O people," he answered, "but have been sent by the Lord of all the worlds.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: He said, “O my people! I do not have any concern with foolishness and I am in fact a Noble Messenger from the Lord Of The Creation.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Said he, 'My people, there is no folly in me; but I am a Messenger from the Lord of all Being.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: He said: O my people! not with me is folly, but I am an apostle from the Lord of the worlds.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: (Hud) said: "O my people! There is no foolishness in me, but (I am) a Messenger from the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists)!

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He said, “O my people! There is no foolishness in me, but I am a messenger from the Lord of the Worlds.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: He said: 'O my people! There is no folly in me; rather I am a Messenger from the Lord of the universe.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: (Hud) said: "O my people! There is no foolishness in me, but (I am) a Messenger from the Lord of all that exists!

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He said: O my people! There is no foolishness in me, but I am a messenger from the Lord of the Worlds.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He said, ‘O my people, I am not in folly. Rather, I am an apostle from the Lord of all the worlds.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: 'My nation, there is no foolishness in me' he replied. 'I am a Messenger of the Lord of the Worlds

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [Hud] said, "O my people, there is not foolishness in me, but I am a messenger from the Lord of the worlds."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: He replied, "My people, I am not a fool but a Messenger of the Lord of the Universe.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He said: O my people! there is no folly in me, but I am an apostle of the Lord of the worlds.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: He said, "My people, I am by no means a fool, but rather am a messenger from the Lord of the Universe,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: He said: "O my people! I am no imbecile, but (I am) a messenger from the Lord and Cherisher of the worlds!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qala ya qawmi laysa bee safahatun walakinnee rasoolun min rabbi alAAalameena

68.arabic: أُبَلِّغُكُمْ رِسَـٰلَـٰتِ رَبِّى وَأَنَا۠ لَكُمْ نَاصِحٌ أَمِينٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: I bring to you the messages of my Lord. I am your sincere friend.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “I convey to you the messages of my Lord and am your trustworthy well-wisher.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: I deliver to you the Messages of my Lord; I am your adviser sincere, faithful.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: I preach unto you the messages of my Lord, and I am unto you a counseller faithful.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "I convey unto you the Messages of my Lord, and I am a trustworthy adviser (or well-wisher) for you.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “I convey to you the messages of my Lord, and I am a trustworthy adviser to you.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: I convey to you the messages of my Lord, and I give you sincere advice.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "I convey unto you the Messages of my Lord, and I am a trustworthy adviser (or well-wisher) for you.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: I convey unto you the messages of my Lord and am for you a true adviser.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: I communicate to you the messages of my Lord and I am a trustworthy well-wisher for you.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: I deliver to you the messages of my Lord and I am your honest adviser.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: I convey to you the messages of my Lord, and I am to you a trustworthy adviser.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: I preach the message of my Lord to you and am a trustworthy advisor for you.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: I deliver to you the messages of my Lord and I am a faithful adviser to you:

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: I am conveying my Lord's messages to you and I am your sincere and honest adviser.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "I but fulfil towards you the duties of my Lord's mission: I am to you a sincere and trustworthy adviser.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Oballighukum risalati rabbee waana lakum nasihun ameenun

69.arabic: أَوَعَجِبْتُمْ أَن جَآءَكُمْ ذِكْرٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ عَلَىٰ رَجُلٍ مِّنكُمْ لِيُنذِرَكُمْ ۚ وَٱذْكُرُوٓا۟ إِذْ جَعَلَكُمْ خُلَفَآءَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ قَوْمِ نُوحٍ وَزَادَكُمْ فِى ٱلْخَلْقِ بَصْۜطَةً ۖ فَٱذْكُرُوٓا۟ ءَالَآءَ ٱللَّهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Do you wonder that a warning has come to you from your Lord through a man who is one of you and warns you? Remember, He made you leaders after the people of Noah, and gave you a greater increase in your stature. So think of the favours of God; you may haply be blessed."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “And are you surprised that an advice came to you from your Lord through a man amongst you, so that he may warn you? Remember when He made you the successors of Nooh’s people, and enlarged your bodies; therefore remember Allah’s favours, so that you may attain good.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: What, do you wonder that a reminder from your Lord should come to you by the lips of a man from among you? That he may warn you; and remember when He appointed you as successors after the people of Noah, and increased you in stature broadly; remember God's bounties; haply you will prosper.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Marvel ye that an admonition from your Lord should come unto you upon a Man from amongst you in order that he may warn you? Remember what time He made you successors after the people, of Nuh, and increased you amply in stature Remember the benefits of Allah, that haply ye may fare well.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "Do you wonder that there has come to you a Reminder (and an advice) from your Lord through a man from amongst you that he may warn you? And remember that He made you successors after the people of Nuh (Noah), and increased you amply in stature. So remember the graces (bestowed upon you) from Allah, so that you may be successful."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “Are you surprised that a reminder has come to you from your Lord, through a man from among you, to warn you? Remember how He made you successors after the people of Noah, and increased you greatly in stature. And remember God’s blessings, so that you may prosper.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Do you wonder that an exhortation should come to you from your Lord through a man from amongst yourselves that he may warn you? And do call to mind when He made you successors after the people of Noah and amply increased you in stature. Remember then the wondrous bounties of Allah, that you may prosper.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "Do you wonder that there has come to you a Reminder (and an advice) from your Lord through a man from among you to warn you And remember that He made you successors (generations after generations) after the people of Nuh and increased you amply in stature. So remember the graces (bestowed upon you) from Allah so that you may be successful."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Marvel ye that there should come unto you a Reminder from your Lord by means of a man among you, that he may warn you? Remember how He made you viceroys after Noah's folk, and gave you growth of stature. Remember (all) the bounties of your Lord, that haply ye may be successful.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Do you consider it odd that there should come to you a reminder from your Lord through a man from among yourselves, so that he may warn you? Remember when He made you successors after the people of Noah, and increased you vastly in creation. So remember Allah’s bounties so that you may be felicitous.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Do you wonder that a remembrance should come to you from your Lord to a man from yourselves in order that he may warn you? Remember, that He has made you the heirs of Noah's nation and increased you in broad stature, remember the favors of Allah in order that you prosper'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Then do you wonder that there has come to you a reminder from your Lord through a man from among you, that he may warn you? And remember when He made you successors after the people of Noah and increased you in stature extensively. So remember the favors of Allah that you might succeed.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Does it seem strange to you that a reminder from your Lord should be sent to a man among you so that He may warn you? Recall when God appointed you as successors of the people of Noah and increased your power over other people. Give thanks to God for His blessings so that perhaps you will have everlasting happiness."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: What! do you wonder that a reminder has come to you from your Lord through a man from among you that he might warn you? And remember when He made you successors after Nuh's people and increased you in excellence in respect of make; therefore remember the benefits of Allah, that you may be successful.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Do you find it strange that a Message should come from your Lord, through one of your own men, to warn you? Remember how He made you successors of Noah's people, and increased you greatly in stature. Remember the favours of God, so that you may prosper."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "Do ye wonder that there hath come to you a message from your Lord through a man of your own people, to warn you? call in remembrance that He made you inheritors after the people of Noah, and gave you a stature tall among the nations. Call in remembrance the benefits (ye have received) from Allah: that so ye may prosper."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: AwaAAajibtum an jaakum thikrun min rabbikum AAala rajulin minkum liyunthirakum waothkuroo ith jaAAalakum khulafaa min baAAdi qawmi noohin wazadakum fee alkhalqi bastatan faothkuroo alaa Allahi laAAallakum tuflihoona

70.arabic: قَالُوٓا۟ أَجِئْتَنَا لِنَعْبُدَ ٱللَّهَ وَحْدَهُۥ وَنَذَرَ مَا كَانَ يَعْبُدُ ءَابَآؤُنَا ۖ فَأْتِنَا بِمَا تَعِدُنَآ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They answered: "Have you come to say to us that we should worship only one God, abandoning those our ancestors had worshipped? If so, bring on us what you threaten us with, if what you say is true."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: They said, “Have you come to us in order that we worship only Allah, and abandon those whom our ancestors worshipped?! So bring upon us what you promise us, if you are truthful.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: They said, 'Why, hast thou come to us that we may serve God alone, and forsake that our fathers served? Then bring us that thou promisest us, if thou speakest truly.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They said: art thou come unto us that we should worship God alone and leave that which our fathers were wont to worship? Bring thou then upon us that wherewith thou threatenest us if thou sayest sooth.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They said: "You have come to us that we should worship Allah Alone and forsake that which our fathers used to worship. So bring us that wherewith you have threatened us if you are of the truthful."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They said, “Did you come to us to make us worship God alone, and abandon what our ancestors used to worship? Then bring us what you threaten us with, if you are truthful.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They said: 'Have you come to us that we should worship none other than Allah and forsake all whom our forefathers were wont to worship? Then bring upon us the scourge with which you have threatened us if you are truthful?'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They said: "You have come to us that we should worship Allah Alone and forsake that which our fathers used to worship. So bring us that wherewith you have threatened us if you are of the truthful."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: They said: Hast come unto us that we should serve Allah alone, and forsake what our fathers worshipped? Then bring upon us that wherewith thou threatenest us if thou art of the truthful!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They said, ‘Have you come to [tell] us that we should worship Allah alone and abandon what our fathers have been worshiping? Then bring us what you threaten us with, should you be truthful.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: They said: 'Have you come to us in order that we worship Allah alone and renounce that which our fathers worshipped? Bring down that with which you threaten us if you are from the truthful!

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: They said, "Have you come to us that we should worship Allah alone and leave what our fathers have worshipped? Then bring us what you promise us, if you should be of the truthful."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They said, "Have you come to make us worship God alone and give up what our fathers worshipped? If you are truthful, let the torment with which you have threatened us, strike us".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They said: Have you come to us that we may serve Allah alone and give up what our fathers used to serve? Then bring to us what you threaten us with, if you are of the truthful ones.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They said, "Have you come to tell us to serve God alone and to forsake the gods our forefathers served? Bring us then what you threaten us with, if you are truthful."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They said: "Comest thou to us, that we may worship Allah alone, and give up the cult of our fathers? bring us what thou threatenest us with, if so be that thou tellest the truth!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qaloo ajitana linaAAbuda Allaha wahdahu wanathara ma kana yaAAbudu abaona fatina bima taAAiduna in kunta mina alssadiqeena

71.arabic: قَالَ قَدْ وَقَعَ عَلَيْكُم مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ رِجْسٌ وَغَضَبٌ ۖ أَتُجَـٰدِلُونَنِى فِىٓ أَسْمَآءٍ سَمَّيْتُمُوهَآ أَنتُمْ وَءَابَآؤُكُم مَّا نَزَّلَ ٱللَّهُ بِهَا مِن سُلْطَـٰنٍ ۚ فَٱنتَظِرُوٓا۟ إِنِّى مَعَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْمُنتَظِرِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: He replied: "You have already been beset with punishment and the wrath of God. Why dispute with me about names invented by you and your ancestors for which no sanction was sent down? So wait (for what is to come), I am waiting with you."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: He said, “Indeed the punishment and the wrath of your Lord have fallen upon you; what! You needlessly dispute with me regarding the names you and your ancestors have fabricated? Allah has not sent down any proof concerning them; therefore wait – I too await with you.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Said he, 'Anger and wrath from your Lord have fallen upon you. What, do you dispute with me regarding names you have named, you and your fathers, touching which God has sent down never authority? Then watch and wait; I shall be with you watching and waiting.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: He said: surely there have befallen you wrath and indignation from your Lord. Dispute ye with me over names ye have named, ye and your fathers, for which Allah hath sent down no warranty? Wait then; I also will be with you of those who wait.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: (Hud) said: "Torment and wrath have already fallen on you from your Lord. Dispute you with me over names which you have named - you and your fathers, with no authority from Allah? Then wait, I am with you among those who wait."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He said, “Condemnation and wrath have befallen you from your Lord. Are you arguing with me over names, which you and your ancestors invented, for which God sent down no authority? Just wait; I am waiting with you.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Hud warned them: 'Surely punishment and wrath from your Lord have befallen upon you. Do you dispute with me about mere names that you and your forefathers have concocted and for which Allah has sent down no sanction? Wait, then, and I too am with you among those who wait.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: (Hud) said: "Rijs (torment) and wrath have already fallen on you from your Lord. Dispute you with me over names which you have named -- you and your fathers -- with no authority from Allah Then wait, I am with you among those who wait."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He said: Terror and wrath from your Lord have already fallen on you. Would ye wrangle with me over names which ye have named, ye and your fathers, for which no warrant from Allah hath been revealed? Then await (the consequence), lo! I (also) am of those awaiting (it).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He said, ‘Punishment and wrath from your Lord has become due against you. Do you dispute with me regarding names, which you have named—you and your fathers—for which Allah has not sent down any authority? So wait! I too am waiting along with you.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: He answered: 'The punishment and wrath of your Lord has already fallen upon you. Would you dispute with me about names which you and your fathers have invented, and for which no authority has been sent down from Allah? Wait if you will, I am waiting with you'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [Hud] said, "Already have defilement and anger fallen upon you from your Lord. Do you dispute with me concerning [mere] names you have named them, you and your fathers, for which Allah has not sent down any authority? Then wait; indeed, I am with you among those who wait."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: He replied, "You will certainly be afflicted by wickedness and the wrath of God. Do you dispute with me about the names of that which you and your fathers have invented? God has given no authority to those names. Wait for God's decree and I, too, am waiting with you."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He said: Indeed uncleanness and wrath from your Lord have lighted upon you; what! do you dispute with me about names which you and your fathers have given? Allah has not sent any authority for them; wait then, I too with you will be of those who wait.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: He said, "Your Lord's wrath and indignation have already fallen upon you. Would you dispute with me about mere names, which you and your fathers have invented, and for which God has revealed no authority? Wait then if you will: I am waiting alongside you."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: He said: "Punishment and wrath have already come upon you from your Lord: dispute ye with me over names which ye have devised - ye and your fathers,- without authority from Allah? then wait: I am amongst you, also waiting."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qala qad waqaAAa AAalaykum min rabbikum rijsun waghadabun atujadiloonanee fee asmain sammaytumooha antum waabaokum ma nazzala Allahu biha min sultanin faintathiroo innee maAAakum mina almuntathireena

72.arabic: فَأَنجَيْنَـٰهُ وَٱلَّذِينَ مَعَهُۥ بِرَحْمَةٍ مِّنَّا وَقَطَعْنَا دَابِرَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا ۖ وَمَا كَانُوا۟ مُؤْمِنِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Then We saved him and those on his side by Our grace, and destroyed to the very last those who rejected Our signs and denied the truth.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: We therefore rescued him and those with him by a great mercy from Us, and We cut off the lineage of those who denied Our signs – and they were not believers.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: So We delivered him, and those with him, by a mercy from Us; and We cut off the last remnant of those who cried lies to Our signs and were not believers.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Then We delivered him and those with him by a mercy from Us, and We utterly cut off those who belied Our signs, and would not be believers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: So We saved him and those who were with him by a Mercy from Us, and We cut the roots of those who belied Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.), and they were not believers.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: So We saved him and those with him, by mercy from Us, and We cut off the roots of those who rejected Our revelations and were not believers.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Then We delivered Hud and his companions by Our mercy, and We utterly cut off the last remnant of those who called the lie to Our signs and would not believe.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: So We saved him and those who were with him out of mercy from Us, and We severed the roots of those who belied Our Ayat; and they were not believers.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And We saved him and those with him by a mercy from Us, and We cut the root of those who denied Our revelations and were not believers.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Then We delivered him and those who were with him by a mercy from Us, and We rooted out those who denied Our signs and were not faithful.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We saved him and all who were with him through Our Mercy and annihilated those who belied Our verses. They were not believers!

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So We saved him and those with him by mercy from Us. And We eliminated those who denied Our signs, and they were not [at all] believers.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Through Our mercy, We saved him and his companions and destroyed the unbelievers who called Our revelations mere lies.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: So We delivered him and those with him by mercy from Us, and We cut off the last of those who rejected Our communications and were not believers.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Then We saved him and those who were with him, by Our mercy; We annihilated those who denied Our signs and would not believe.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: We saved him and those who adhered to him. By Our mercy, and We cut off the roots of those who rejected Our signs and did not believe.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faanjaynahu waallatheena maAAahu birahmatin minna waqataAAna dabira allatheena kaththaboo biayatina wama kanoo mumineena

73.arabic: وَإِلَىٰ ثَمُودَ أَخَاهُمْ صَـٰلِحًا ۗ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرُهُۥ ۖ قَدْ جَآءَتْكُم بَيِّنَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ ۖ هَـٰذِهِۦ نَاقَةُ ٱللَّهِ لَكُمْ ءَايَةً ۖ فَذَرُوهَا تَأْكُلْ فِىٓ أَرْضِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَلَا تَمَسُّوهَا بِسُوٓءٍ فَيَأْخُذَكُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: We sent to Thamud their brother Saleh. "O you people," said he, "worship God, for you have no other god but He. Clear proof has come to you already from your Lord, and this she-camel of God is the token for you. Leave her free to graze upon God's earth, and do not molest her lest a grievous punishment should befall you.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And We sent Saleh to the tribe of Thamud, from their own community; he said, “O my people! Worship Allah – you do not have any God except Him; indeed a clear proof has come to you from your Lord; this is Allah’s she-camel – a sign for you – so leave her free to feed in Allah’s earth, and do not touch her with evil intentions for a painful punishment will seize you.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And to Thamood their brother Salih; he said, 'O my people, serve God! You have no god other than He; there has now come to you a clear sign from your Lord -- this is the She-camel of God, to be a sign for you. Leave her that she may eat in God's earth, and do not touch her with evil, lest you be seized by a painful chastisement.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And unto Thamud We sent their brother, Salih He said: my people! worship Allah, no god ye have but He; surely there hath come unto you an evidence from your Lord. Yonder is the she-camel of Allah: a sign unto you; so leave her alone, pasturing on Allah's earth, and touch her not with evil, lest there seize you a torment afflictive.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And to Thamud (people, We sent) their brother Salih (Saleh). He said: "O my people! Worship Allah! You have no other Ilah (God) but Him. (La ilaha ill-Allah: none has the right to be worshipped but Allah). Indeed there has come to you a clear sign (the miracle of the coming out of a huge she-camel from the midst of a rock) from your Lord. This she-camel of Allah is a sign unto you; so you leave her to graze in Allah's earth, and touch her not with harm, lest a painful torment should seize you.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And to Thamood, their brother Saleh. He said, “O my people! Worship God; you have no god other than Him. Clarification has come to you from your Lord. This she-camel of God is a sign for you. So leave her to graze on God’s earth, and do her no harm, lest a painful penalty seizes you.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And to Thamud We sent forth their brother. Salih. He said to them: 'O my people! Serve Allah, you have no other god than Him. Truly there has come to you a clear proof from your Lord. This she-camel from Allah is a Divine portent for you. So leave her alone to pasture on Allah's earth, and touch her with no evil lest a painful chastisement should seize you.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And to Thamud (people, We sent) their brother Salih. He said: "O my people! Worship Allah! You have no other god but Him. Indeed there has come to you a clear sign from your Lord. This she-camel of Allah is a sign unto you; so you leave her to graze in Allah's earth, and touch her not with harm, lest a painful torment should seize you.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And to (the tribe of) Thamud (We sent) their brother Salih. He said: O my people! Serve Allah. Ye have no other Allah save Him. A wonder from your Lord hath come unto you. Lo! this is the camel of Allah, a token unto you; so let her feed in Allah's earth, and touch her not with hurt lest painful torment seize you.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And to [the people of] Thamud [We sent] Salih, their brother. He said, ‘O my people, worship Allah! You have no other god besides Him. There has certainly come to you a manifest proof from your Lord. This she-camel of Allah is a sign for you. Let her alone to graze [freely] in Allah’s land, and do not cause her any harm, for then you shall be seized by a painful punishment.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And to Thamood We sent their brother Salih. He said: 'Worship Allah, my nation, for you have no god except He. A clear proof has come to you from your Lord. Here is the shecamel of Allah, a sign for you. Leave her to graze in the earth of Allah and do not touch her with evil, lest a painful punishment seizes you.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And to the Thamud [We sent] their brother Salih. He said, "O my people, worship Allah; you have no deity other than Him. There has come to you clear evidence from your Lord. This is the she-camel of Allah [sent] to you as a sign. So leave her to eat within Allah 's land and do not touch her with harm, lest there seize you a painful punishment.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We sent Salih to his brethren, the tribe of Thamud, who told them, "Worship God your only Lord. Authoritative evidence has come to you from your Lord and this she-camel is the evidence for you from God. Let her graze in the land of God. Do not give her any trouble lest a painful torment will strike you.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And to Samood (We sent) their brother Salih. He said: O my people! serve Allah, you have no god other than Him; clear proof indeed has come to you from your Lord; this is (as) Allah's she-camel for you-- a sign, therefore leave her alone to pasture on Allah's earth, and do not touch her with any harm, otherwise painful chastisement will overtake you.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: To the Thamud We sent their brother Salih. He said, "O my people, worship God; you have no other god but Him. A veritable proof has come to you from your Lord: this is God's she-camel, a sign for you, so let her feed in God's land and do not harm her in any way, or you will be overwhelmed by a painful punishment.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: To the Thamud people (We sent) Salih, one of their own brethren: He said: "O my people! worship Allah: ye have no other god but Him. Now hath come unto you a clear (Sign) from your Lord! This she-camel of Allah is a Sign unto you: So leave her to graze in Allah's earth, and let her come to no harm, or ye shall be seized with a grievous punishment.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waila thamooda akhahum salihan qala ya qawmi oAAbudoo Allaha ma lakum min ilahin ghayruhu qad jaatkum bayyinatun min rabbikum hathihi naqatu Allahi lakum ayatan fatharooha takul fee ardi Allahi wala tamassooha bisooin fayakhuthakum AAathabun aleemun

74.arabic: وَٱذْكُرُوٓا۟ إِذْ جَعَلَكُمْ خُلَفَآءَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ عَادٍ وَبَوَّأَكُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ تَتَّخِذُونَ مِن سُهُولِهَا قُصُورًا وَتَنْحِتُونَ ٱلْجِبَالَ بُيُوتًا ۖ فَٱذْكُرُوٓا۟ ءَالَآءَ ٱللَّهِ وَلَا تَعْثَوْا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مُفْسِدِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Remember, how you were made leaders after the people of 'Ad, and were settled on the land so that you could construct on the plains palaces, and carve dwellings out of mountains. So think of the favours of God, and do not act with corruption in the land."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “And remember when He made you successors of A’ad and gave you a region in the earth, so you now build palaces in the soft plains and carve houses in rocks; therefore remember Allah’s favours and do not roam the earth spreading turmoil.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And remember when He appointed you successors after Ad, and lodged you in the land, taking to yourselves castles of its plains, and hewing its mountains into houses. Remember God's bounties, and do not mischief in the earth, working corruption.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And remember what time He made you successors after 'Ad and inherited you in earth; ye take for yourselves palaces in the plains whereof and ye hew out the mountains as houses. Remember ye wherefore the benefits of Allah and commit not evil on the earth as corrupters.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "And remember when He made you successors after 'Ad (people) and gave you habitations in the land, you build for yourselves palaces in plains, and carve out homes in the mountains. So remember the graces (bestowed upon you) from Allah, and do not go about making mischief on the earth."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “And remember how He made you successors after Aad, and settled you in the land. You make for yourselves mansions on its plains, and carve out dwellings in the mountains. So remember God’s benefits, and do not roam the earth corruptingly.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And call to mind when He made you successors after 'Ad and gave you power in the earth so that you took for yourselves palaces in its plains and hewed out dwellings in the mountains. Remember, then, the wondrous bounties of Allah and do not go about creating mischief in the land.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And remember when He made you successors (generations) after `Ad and gave you habitations in the land, you build for yourselves palaces in plains, and carve out homes in the mountains. So remember the graces (bestowed upon you) from Allah, and do not go about making mischief on the earth.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And remember how He made you viceroys after A'ad and gave you station in the earth. Ye choose castles in the plains and hew the mountains into dwellings. So remember (all) the bounties of Allah and do not evil, making mischief in the earth.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Remember when He made you successors after [the people of] ‘Ad, and settled you in the land: you build palaces in its plains, and hew houses out of the mountains. So remember Allah’s bounties, and do not act wickedly on the earth, causing corruption.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Remember, that He has made you the successor of 'Aad, and lodged you in the land. You have built palaces on its plains and hewed out houses in the mountains. Remember the favor of Allah and do not act mischievously in the earth, corrupting'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And remember when He made you successors after the 'Aad and settled you in the land, [and] you take for yourselves palaces from its plains and carve from the mountains, homes. Then remember the favors of Allah and do not commit abuse on the earth, spreading corruption."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Recall (the time) when We settled you in the land as the heirs of the tribe of Ad and how you established mansions in the plains and carved homes out of the mountains. Give thanks to God for His favors and do not commit evil in the land."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And remember when He made you successors after Ad and settled you in the land-- you make mansions on its plains and hew out houses in the mountains-- remember therefore Allah's benefits and do not act corruptly in the land, making mischief.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Remember when He made you successors to the 'Ad and settled you in the land. You built palaces on its plains and carved houses out of the mountains. Remember God's blessings and do not spread corruption in the land,"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "And remember how He made you inheritors after the 'Ad people and gave you habitations in the land: ye build for yourselves palaces and castles in (open) plains, and carve out homes in the mountains; so bring to remembrance the benefits (ye have received) from Allah, and refrain from evil and mischief on the earth."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waothkuroo ith jaAAalakum khulafaa min baAAdi AAadin wabawwaakum fee alardi tattakhithoona min suhooliha qusooran watanhitoona aljibala buyootan faothkuroo alaa Allahi wala taAAthaw fee alardi mufsideena

75.arabic: قَالَ ٱلْمَلَأُ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ مِن قَوْمِهِۦ لِلَّذِينَ ٱسْتُضْعِفُوا۟ لِمَنْ ءَامَنَ مِنْهُمْ أَتَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّ صَـٰلِحًا مُّرْسَلٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦ ۚ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا بِمَآ أُرْسِلَ بِهِۦ مُؤْمِنُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The chiefs among the people who were arrogant towards the weaker ones among them who believed, asked: "Do you really know that Saleh has been sent by his Lord?" They said: "Indeed we believe in the message he has brought."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: The proud leaders of his people (mockingly) said to the weak Muslims, “Do you know that Saleh is (really) the Noble Messenger of his Lord?” They said, “We believe in whatever he has been sent with.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Said the Council of those of his people who waxed proud to those that were abased, to those of them who believed, 'Do you know that Salih is an Envoy from his Lord?' They said, 'In the Message he has been sent with we are believers.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: The chiefs of those who were stiff-necked amongst His people said unto those who were counted weak -unto such of them as believed: know ye that Salih is a sent one of his Lord? They said: verily we are believers in that wherewith he hath been sent.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: The leaders of those who were arrogant among his people said to those who were counted weak - to such of them as believed: "Know you that Salih (Saleh) is one sent from his Lord." They said: "We indeed believe in that with which he has been sent."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: The elite of his people, who were arrogant, said to the common people who had believed, “Do you know that Saleh is sent from his Lord?” They said, “We are believers in what he was sent with.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: The haughty elders of his people said to those believers who had been oppressed: 'Do you know that Salih is one sent forth with a message from his Lord?' They, replied: 'Surely we believe in the message with which he has been sent.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: The leaders of those who were arrogant among his people said to those who were counted weak -- to such of them as believed: "Know you that Salih is one sent from his Lord." They said: "We indeed believe in that with which he has been sent."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: The chieftains of his people, who were scornful, said unto those whom they despised, unto such of them as believed: Know ye that Salih is one sent from his Lord? They said: Lo! In that wherewith he hath been sent we are believers.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: The elite of his people who were arrogant said to those who were oppressed—to those among them who had faith—‘Do you know that Salih has been sent by his Lord?’ They said, ‘We indeed believe in what he has been sent with.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: The proud assembly of his nation said to the weak? amongst them who believed: 'Do you really believe that Salih is sent from his Lord' They answered: 'We believe in that with which he was sent'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Said the eminent ones who were arrogant among his people to those who were oppressed - to those who believed among them, "Do you [actually] know that Salih is sent from his Lord?" They said, "Indeed we, in that with which he was sent, are believers."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The proud ones among Salih's people asked his oppressed followers, "Do you (really) know that Salih is a Messenger of his Lord?" They replied, "We have faith in the Message which he preaches".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: The chief of those who behaved proudly among his people said to those who were considered weak, to those who believed from among them: Do you know that Salih is sent by his Lord? They said: Surely we are believers in what he has been sent with

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: but the arrogant leaders of his people said to the believers who were deemed weak, "Do you know for certain that Salih is one sent from his Lord?" They replied, "We believe in the message which has been sent through him."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: The leaders of the arrogant party among his people said to those who were reckoned powerless - those among them who believed: "know ye indeed that Salih is a messenger from his Lord?" They said: "We do indeed believe in the revelation which hath been sent through him."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qala almalao allatheena istakbaroo min qawmihi lillatheena istudAAifoo liman amana minhum ataAAlamoona anna salihan mursalun min rabbihi qaloo inna bima orsila bihi muminoona

76.arabic: قَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱسْتَكْبَرُوٓا۟ إِنَّا بِٱلَّذِىٓ ءَامَنتُم بِهِۦ كَـٰفِرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Those who were arrogant answered: "We do not believe in what you believe."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: The proud ones said, “We deny what you have believed in.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Said the ones who waxed proud, 'As for us, we are unbelievers in the thing in which you believe.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Those who were stiff-necked said: verily we are disbelievers in that which ye believe.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Those who were arrogant said: "Verily, we disbelieve in that which you believe in."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those who were arrogant said, “We reject what you believe in.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: The haughty ones remarked. 'Most certainly we disbelieve in that which you believe.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Those who were arrogant said: "Verily, we disbelieve in that which you believe in."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Those who were scornful said: Lo! in that which ye believe we are disbelievers.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Those who were arrogant said, ‘We indeed disbelieve in what you have believed.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Those who were proud said: 'We disbelieve in that which you believed in'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Said those who were arrogant, "Indeed we, in that which you have believed, are disbelievers."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The proud oppressors said, "We reject that which you believe in".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Those who were haughty said: Surely we are deniers of what you believe in.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: The arrogant leaders said, "We reject what you believe in."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: The Arrogant party said: "For our part, we reject what ye believe in."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qala allatheena istakbaroo inna biallathee amantum bihi kafiroona

77.arabic: فَعَقَرُوا۟ ٱلنَّاقَةَ وَعَتَوْا۟ عَنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّهِمْ وَقَالُوا۟ يَـٰصَـٰلِحُ ٱئْتِنَا بِمَا تَعِدُنَآ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Then they hamstrung the she-camel and rebelled against the command of their Lord, and said: "Bring, O Saleh, on us the affliction you promise, if you are one of the sent ones."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So they hamstrung the she-camel and rebelled against the command of their Lord and said, “O Saleh! Bring upon us what you promise us, if you are a Noble Messenger.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: So they hamstrung the She-camel and turned in disdain from the commandment of their Lord, saying, 'O Salih, bring us that thou promisest us, if thou art an Envoy.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Then they hamstrung the she camel and disdained the commandment of their Lord, and said: Salih! bring upon us that wherewith thou hast threatened us if thou art in sooth of the sent ones.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: So they killed the she-camel and insolently defied the Commandment of their Lord, and said: "O Salih (Saleh)! Bring about your threats if you are indeed one of the Messengers (of Allah)."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: So they hamstrung the she-camel, and defied the command of their Lord, and said, “O Saleh, bring upon us what you threaten us with, if you are one of the messengers.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Then they hamstrung the she-camel, disdainfuliy disobeyed the commandment of their Lord, and said: 'O Salih! Bring upon us the scourge with which you threatened us if you are truly a Messenger [of Allah].'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: So they killed the she-camel and insolently defied the commandment of their Lord, and said: "O Salih! Bring about your threats if you are indeed one of the Messengers (of Allah)."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: So they hamstrung the she-camel, and they flouted the commandment of their Lord, and they said: O Salih! Bring upon us that thou threatenest if thou art indeed of those sent (from Allah).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So they hamstrung the She-camel and defied the command of their Lord, and they said, ‘O Salih, bring us what you threaten us with, if you are one of the apostles.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: (Then) they hamstrung the shecamel and defied the order of their Lord, saying to Salih: 'Bring down that which you have promised us if you truly are one of the Messengers'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So they hamstrung the she-camel and were insolent toward the command of their Lord and said, "O Salih, bring us what you promise us, if you should be of the messengers."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They then slew the camel and rebelled against the orders of their Lord saying, " Salih, if you are truly a Messenger, let that torment with which you have threatened us come to pass."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: So they slew the she-camel and revolted against their Lord's commandment, and they said: O Salih! bring us what you threatened us with, if you are one of the apostles.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: So they hamstrung the she-camel, and insolently defied the commandment of their Lord, saying, "O Salih! Bring upon us what you threaten us with if you are indeed a messenger."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Then they ham-strung the she-camel, and insolently defied the order of their Lord, saying: "O Salih! bring about thy threats, if thou art a messenger (of Allah)!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: FaAAaqaroo alnnaqata waAAataw AAan amri rabbihim waqaloo ya salihu itina bima taAAiduna in kunta mina almursaleena

78.arabic: فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ ٱلرَّجْفَةُ فَأَصْبَحُوا۟ فِى دَارِهِمْ جَـٰثِمِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Then they were seized by an earthquake, and lay overturned on the ground in their homes in the morning.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Therefore the earthquake seized them, so at morning they remained lying flattened in their homes.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: So the earthquake seized them, and morning found them in their habitation fallen prostrate.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Whereupon an earthquake seized them, so that they lay prone in their dwellings.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: So the earthquake seized them, and they lay (dead), prostrate in their homes.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Whereupon the quake overtook them, and they became lifeless bodies in their homes.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Thereupon a shocking catastrophe seized them, so that they lay prostrate in their dwellings.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: So the earthquake seized them, and they lay (dead), prostrate in their homes.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: So the earthquake seized them, and morning found them prostrate in their dwelling-place.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Thereupon the earthquake seized them, and they lay lifeless prostrate in their homes.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Thereupon the earthquake seized them, at morning in their dwellings they were crouched, dead.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So the earthquake seized them, and they became within their home [corpses] fallen prone.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Suddenly, an earthquake jolted them and they were left motionless in their homes.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Then the earthquake overtook them, so they became motionless bodies in their abode.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: So the earthquake overwhelmed them, and morning found them prostrate in their dwelling places.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: So the earthquake took them unawares, and they lay prostrate in their homes in the morning!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faakhathathumu alrrajfatu faasbahoo fee darihim jathimeena

79.arabic: فَتَوَلَّىٰ عَنْهُمْ وَقَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ لَقَدْ أَبْلَغْتُكُمْ رِسَالَةَ رَبِّى وَنَصَحْتُ لَكُمْ وَلَـٰكِن لَّا تُحِبُّونَ ٱلنَّـٰصِحِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Saleh turned away from them and said: "O my people, I conveyed to you the message of my Lord and warned you; but you do not like those who wish you well."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Saleh therefore turned away from them and said, “O my people! Indeed I did deliver my Lord’s message to you and wished you good, but you do not want well-wishers.” (The people in the graves can hear the speech of those who are on earth.)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: So he turned his back on them, and said, 'O my people, I have delivered to you the Message of my Lord, and advised you sincerely; but you do not love sincere advisers.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Then he turned from them, and said: O my people! assuredly delivered unto you the messages of my Lord, and counselled you good, but ye approve not the good counsellors.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then he [Salih (Saleh)] turned from them, and said: "O my people! I have indeed conveyed to you the Message of my Lord, and have given you good advice but you like not good advisers."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Then he turned away from them, and said, “O my people, I have delivered to you the message of my Lord, and I have advised you, but you do not like those who give advice.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And Salih left them, saying: 'O my people! I conveyed to you the message of my Lord and gave you good advice; but you have no liking for your well-wishers.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
