translations.text: Then he [Salih] turned from them, and said: "O my people! I have indeed conveyed to you the Message of my Lord, and have given you good advice, but you like not good advisers."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And (Salih) turned from them and said: O my people! I delivered my Lord's message unto you and gave you good advice, but ye love not good advisers.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So he abandoned them [to their fate], and said, ‘O my people! Certainly I communicated to you the message of my Lord, and I was your well-wisher, but you did not like well-wishers.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: He turned from them, saying: 'I conveyed to you, my nation the Message of my Lord and gave you counsel; but you had no love for sincere advisers'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And he turned away from them and said, "O my people, I had certainly conveyed to you the message of my Lord and advised you, but you do not like advisors."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: He turned away from them saying, "My people, I preached the Message of my Lord to you and gave you good advice, but you do not love advisors."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Then he turned away from them and said: O my people I did certainly deliver to you the message of my Lord, and I gave you good advice, but you do not love those who give good advice.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: He left them, saying, "My people, I delivered my Lord's messages to you and counselled you sincerely, but you do not like sincere advisors."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: So Salih left them, saying: "O my people! I did indeed convey to you the message for which I was sent by my Lord: I gave you good counsel, but ye love not good counsellors!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fatawalla AAanhum waqala ya qawmi laqad ablaghtukum risalata rabbee wanasahtu lakum walakin la tuhibboona alnnasiheena

80.arabic: وَلُوطًا إِذْ قَالَ لِقَوْمِهِۦٓ أَتَأْتُونَ ٱلْفَـٰحِشَةَ مَا سَبَقَكُم بِهَا مِنْ أَحَدٍ مِّنَ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And We sent Lot, who said to his people: "Why do you commit this lecherous act which none in the world has committed before?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And We sent Lut – when he said to his people, “What! You commit the shameful acts which no one in the creation has ever done before you?”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And Lot, when he said to his people, 'What, do you commit such indecency as never any being in all the world committed before you?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And We sent Lut, when he said unto his people: commit ye an indecency wherewith none hath preceded you in the worlds?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And (remember) Lout (Lot), when he said to his people: "Do you commit the worst sin such as none preceding you has committed in the 'Alamin (mankind and jinns)?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And Lot, when he said to his people, “Do you commit lewdness no people anywhere have ever committed before you?”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And remember when We sent Lot [as a Messeng to his people and he said to them: 'Do you realize you practise an indecency of which no other people in the world were guilty of before you?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And (remember) Lut, when he said to his people: "Do you commit lewdness such as none preceding you has committed in all of the nations

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And Lot! (Remember) when he said unto his folk: Will ye commit abomination such as no creature ever did before you?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And Lot, when he said to his people, ‘What! Do you commit an outrage none in the world ever committed before you?!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And Lot, who said to his nation: 'Do you commit such indecency (sodomy) in a way that no one has preceded you in the worlds?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And [We had sent] Lot when he said to his people, "Do you commit such immorality as no one has preceded you with from among the worlds?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Lot told his people, "Why do you commit such indecent acts that have never been committed by anyone before?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And (We sent) Lut when he said to his people: What! do you commit an indecency which any one in the world has not done before you?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We sent Lot, who said to his people, "How can you commit an abomination such as no one in the world has ever done before you?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: We also (sent) Lut: He said to his people: "Do ye commit lewdness such as no people in creation (ever) committed before you?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walootan ith qala liqawmihi atatoona alfahishata ma sabaqakum biha min ahadin mina alAAalameena

81.arabic: إِنَّكُمْ لَتَأْتُونَ ٱلرِّجَالَ شَهْوَةً مِّن دُونِ ٱلنِّسَآءِ ۚ بَلْ أَنتُمْ قَوْمٌ مُّسْرِفُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: In preference to women you satisfy your lust with men. Indeed you are a people who are guilty of excess."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “You lustfully go towards men, instead of women! In fact, you have transgressed the limits.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: See, you approach men lustfully instead of women; no, you are a people that do exceed.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily ye go in lustfully unto men instead of women! Aye! ye are a people extravagant.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "Verily, you practise your lusts on men instead of women. Nay, but you are a people transgressing beyond bounds (by committing great sins)."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “You lust after men rather than women. You are an excessive people.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: You approach men lustfully in place of women. You are a people who exceed all bounds.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "Verily, you practice your lusts on men instead of women. Nay, but you are a people transgressing beyond bounds."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! ye come with lust unto men instead of women. Nay, but ye are wanton folk.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed you come to men with desire instead of women! Indeed, you are a profligate lot.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: You approach men lustfully instead of women. Truly, you are a nation who exceed (in sin)'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, you approach men with desire, instead of women. Rather, you are a transgressing people."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: You engage in lustful activities with people instead of women. You have become transgressing people.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Most surely you come to males in lust besides females; nay you are an extravagant people.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: You lust after men rather than women! You transgress all bounds!"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "For ye practise your lusts on men in preference to women: ye are indeed a people transgressing beyond bounds."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Innakum latatoona alrrijala shahwatan min dooni alnnisai bal antum qawmun musrifoona

82.arabic: وَمَا كَانَ جَوَابَ قَوْمِهِۦٓ إِلَّآ أَن قَالُوٓا۟ أَخْرِجُوهُم مِّن قَرْيَتِكُمْ ۖ إِنَّهُمْ أُنَاسٌ يَتَطَهَّرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: His people made no answer, and only said: "Drive them out of the city. They profess to be pure."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And his people had no answer except to say, “Turn them out of your dwellings; these are people who wish purity!”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And the only answer of his people was that they said, 'Expel them from your city; surely they are folk that keep themselves clean!'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And naught was the answer of his people save that they said: drive themforth from your city, verily they are a people who would be pure!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And the answer of his people was only that they said: "Drive them out of your town, these are indeed men who want to be pure (from sins)!"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And his people's only answer was to say, “Expel them from your town; they are purist people.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Their only answer was: 'Banish them from your town. They are a people who pretend to be pure.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And the answer of his people was only that they said: "Drive them out of your town, these are indeed men who want to be pure (from sins)!"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And the answer of his people was only that they said (one to another): Turn them out of your township. They are folk, forsooth, who keep pure.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: But the only answer of his people was that they said, ‘Expel them from your town! They are indeed a puritanical lot.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: The only answer of his nation was: 'Expel them from your village. They are people who keep themselves purified'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: But the answer of his people was only that they said, "Evict them from your city! Indeed, they are men who keep themselves pure."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: His people had no answer to his remarks but to tell one another, "Expel him from our town; he and his people want to purify themselves."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And the answer of his people was no other than that they said: Turn them out of your town, surely they are a people who seek to purify (themselves).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: The only answer given by his people was, "Turn them out of your town. They are people who regard themselves to be pure."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And his people gave no answer but this: they said, "Drive them out of your city: these are indeed men who want to be clean and pure!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wama kana jawaba qawmihi illa an qaloo akhrijoohum min qaryatikum innahum onasun yatatahharoona

83.arabic: فَأَنجَيْنَـٰهُ وَأَهْلَهُۥٓ إِلَّا ٱمْرَأَتَهُۥ كَانَتْ مِنَ ٱلْغَـٰبِرِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But We saved him and his family, except for his wife who was one of those who stayed behind.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And We rescued him and his family, except his wife – she became of those who stayed behind.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: So We delivered him and his family, except his wife; she was one of those that tarried.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Then We delivered him and his household, save his wife: she was among the lingerers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then We saved him and his family, except his wife; she was of those who remained behind (in the torment).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: But We saved him and his family, except for his wife; she was of those who lagged behind.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Then We delivered Lot and his household save his wife who stayed behind,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then We saved him and his family, except his wife; she was of the Ghabirin (those who lagged behind).

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And We rescued him and his household, save his wife, who was of those who stayed behind.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Thereupon We delivered him and his family, except his wife; she was one of those who remained behind.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We saved him and all his family, except his wife, who was made to remain,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So We saved him and his family, except for his wife; she was of those who remained [with the evildoers].

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We saved (Lot) and his family except his wife who remained with the rest.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: So We delivered him and his followers, except his wife; she was of those who remained behind.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: So We saved him and his family -- except for his wife. She was one of those who stayed behind.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: But we saved him and his family, except his wife: she was of those who legged behind.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faanjaynahu waahlahu illa imraatahu kanat mina alghabireena

84.arabic: وَأَمْطَرْنَا عَلَيْهِم مَّطَرًا ۖ فَٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And We rained down on them a shower (of Stones). So witness the end of sinners!

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And We rained a shower (of stones) upon them; therefore see what sort of fate befell the culprits!

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And We rained down upon them a rain; so behold thou, how was the end of the sinners!

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And We rained upon them a rain. So behold! what like was the end of the sinners.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And We rained down on them a rain (of stones). Then see what was the end of the Mujrimun (criminals, polytheists, sinners, etc.).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And We rained down on them a rain; note the consequences for the sinners.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and We let loose a shower [of stones] upon them, Observe, then, the end of the evil-doers.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And We rained down on them a rain (of stones). Then see what was the end of the criminals.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And We rained a rain upon them. See now the nature of the consequence of evil-doers!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Then We poured down upon them a rain [of stones]. So observe how was the fate of the guilty!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: and We rained down upon them a rain. So look how was the end of the evildoers.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And We rained upon them a rain [of stones]. Then see how was the end of the criminals.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We sent a torrential rain unto the (unbelievers). Consider how disastrous the end of the criminals was!

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And We rained upon them a rain; consider then what was the end of the guilty.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We rained down on them a shower [of brimstone]. Then see what was the end of the evil-doers.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And we rained down on them a shower (of brimstone): Then see what was the end of those who indulged in sin and crime!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waamtarna AAalayhim mataran faonthur kayfa kana AAaqibatu almujrimeena

85.arabic: وَإِلَىٰ مَدْيَنَ أَخَاهُمْ شُعَيْبًا ۗ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرُهُۥ ۖ قَدْ جَآءَتْكُم بَيِّنَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ ۖ فَأَوْفُوا۟ ٱلْكَيْلَ وَٱلْمِيزَانَ وَلَا تَبْخَسُوا۟ ٱلنَّاسَ أَشْيَآءَهُمْ وَلَا تُفْسِدُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ بَعْدَ إِصْلَـٰحِهَا ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Remember, We sent to Midian their brother Shu'aib. "O you people," he said, "worship God, for you have no other god but He. Clear proof has come to you from your Lord; so give in full measure and full weight; do not keep back from people what is theirs, and do not corrupt the land after it has been reformed. This is best for you if you believe.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And We sent Shuaib to Madyan from their community; he said, “O my people! Worship Allah – you do not have any God except Him; indeed a clear proof has come to you from your Lord, so measure and weigh in full and do not give the people their goods diminished, and do not spread turmoil in the earth after it is organised; this is for your good, if you believe.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And to Midian their brother Shuaib; he said, 'O my people, serve God! You have no god other than He; there has now come to you a clear sign from your Lord. So fill up the measure and the balance, and diminish not the goods of the people; and do not corruption in the land, after it has been set right; that is better for you, if you are believers.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And unto Madyan We sent their brother Shu'aib. He said: O my people! worship Allah, no god ye have buthe; surely there hath come unto you an evidence from your Lord. So give full measure and weight, and defraud not people of their things, and act not corruptly on the earth after the ordering thereof; thatis best for you if ye be believers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And to (the people of) Madyan (Midian), (We sent) their brother Shu'aib. He said: "O my people! Worship Allah! You have no other Ilah (God) but Him. [La ilaha ill-Allah (none has the right to be worshipped but Allah)]." Verily, a clear proof (sign) from your Lord has come unto you; so give full measure and full weight and wrong not men in their things, and do not mischief on the earth after it has been set in order, that will be better for you, if you are believers.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And to Median, their brother Shuaib. He said, “O my people, worship God; you have no god other than Him. A clear proof has come to you from your Lord. Give full measure and weight, and do not cheat people out of their rights, and do not corrupt the land once it has been set right. This is better for you, if you are believers.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And to Midian We sent forth their brother Shu'ayb He exhorted them: O my people! Serve Allah, you have no god but Him. Indeed a clear proof has come to you from your Lord. So give just weight and measure and diminish not to men their things ' and make no mischief on the earth after it has been set in good order. That is to your own good, if you truly believe.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And to (the people of) Madyan, (We sent) their brother Shu`ayb. He said: "O my people! Worship Allah! You have no other God but Him. Verily, a clear proof (sign) from your Lord has come unto you; so give full measure and full weight and wrong not men in their things, and do not do mischief on the earth after it has been set in order, that will be better for you, if you are believers.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And unto Midian (We sent) their brother, Shu'eyb. He said: O my people! Serve Allah. Ye have no other Allah save Him. Lo! a clear proof hath come unto you from your Lord; so give full measure and full weight and wrong not mankind in their goods, and work not confusion in the earth after the fair ordering thereof. That will be better for you, if ye are believers.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And to [the people of] Midian [We sent] Shu‘ayb, their brother. He said, ‘O my people, worship Allah! You have no other god besides Him. There has certainly come to you a manifest proof from your Lord. Observe fully the measure and the balance, and do not cheat the people of their goods, and do not cause corruption on the earth after its restoration. That is better for you, if you are faithful.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And to Midian, their brother Shu'aib. He said: 'Worship Allah, my nation, for you have no god except He. A clear sign has come to you from your Lord. Give just weight, and full measure; and do not diminish the goods of people. Do not corrupt the land after it has been set right, that is best for you, if you are believers.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And to [the people of] Madyan [We sent] their brother Shu'ayb. He said, "O my people, worship Allah; you have no deity other than Him. There has come to you clear evidence from your Lord. So fulfill the measure and weight and do not deprive people of their due and cause not corruption upon the earth after its reformation. That is better for you, if you should be believers.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We sent to the people of Midian their brother Shu'ayb who also told his people to worship God their only Lord. He said, "A guidance has come to you from your Lord. Maintain proper measures and weights in trade. Do not cause any deficiency in people's property or destroy the land after it has been reformed. This is for your own good, if you have any faith.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And to Madyan (We sent) their brother Shu'aib. He said: O my people! serve Allah, you have no god other than Him; clear proof indeed has come to you from your Lord, therefore give full measure and weight and do not diminish to men their things, and do not make mischief in the land after its reform; this is better for you if you are believers:

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: To Midian We sent their brother Shu'ayb. He said, "O my people, worship God; you have no other god but Him. A clear Sign has indeed come to you from your Lord. So give full measure and full weight, and do not deliver short. Do not corrupt the land after it has been set in order. This is for your own good, if you are true believers.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: To the Madyan people We sent Shu'aib, one of their own brethren: he said: "O my people! worship Allah; Ye have no other god but Him. Now hath come unto you a clear (Sign) from your Lord! Give just measure and weight, nor withhold from the people the things that are their due; and do no mischief on the earth after it has been set in order: that will be best for you, if ye have Faith.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waila madyana akhahum shuAAayban qala ya qawmi oAAbudoo Allaha ma lakum min ilahin ghayruhu qad jaatkum bayyinatun min rabbikum faawfoo alkayla waalmeezana wala tabkhasoo alnnasa ashyaahum wala tufsidoo fee alardi baAAda islahiha thalikum khayrun lakum in kuntum mumineena

86.arabic: وَلَا تَقْعُدُوا۟ بِكُلِّ صِرَٰطٍ تُوعِدُونَ وَتَصُدُّونَ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ مَنْ ءَامَنَ بِهِۦ وَتَبْغُونَهَا عِوَجًا ۚ وَٱذْكُرُوٓا۟ إِذْ كُنتُمْ قَلِيلًا فَكَثَّرَكُمْ ۖ وَٱنظُرُوا۟ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلْمُفْسِدِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Do not lie in ambush to intimidate and divert from the path of God those who believe in Him, nor seek obliquity in it. Remember the day when you were few and He increased your numbers. So consider the fate of those who were evil.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “And do not be seated on every road in order to scare the travellers, and to prevent from Allah’s path the people who believe in Him, wishing to distort it; and remember when you were few and He increased your numbers; and see what sort of fate befell the mischievous!”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And do not sit in every path, threatening and barring from God's way those who believe in Him, desiring to make it crooked. And remember when you were few, and He multiplied you; and behold, how was the end of the workers of corruption.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And beset not every highway menacing and turning aside from the path of Allah those who believe in Him, and seeking to make it crooked. And remember when ye were small, and He thereafter multiplied you; and behold what like was the end of the corrupters.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "And sit not on every road, threatening, and hindering from the Path of Allah those who believe in Him. and seeking to make it crooked. And remember when you were but few, and He multiplied you. And see what was the end of the Mufsidun (mischief-makers, corrupts, liars).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “And do not lurk on every path, making threats and turning away from the path of God those who believe in Him, seeking to distort it. And remember how you were few, and how He made you numerous. So note the consequences for the corrupters.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And do not lie in ambush by every path [of life] seeking to overawe or to hinder from the path of Allah those who believe, nor seek to make the path crooked. Remember, how you were once few, and then He multiplied you, and keep in mind what was the end of mischiefmakers.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "And sit not on every road, threatening, and hindering from the path of Allah those who believe in Him, and seeking to make it crooked. And remember when you were but few, and He multiplied you. And see what was the end of the mischief-makers.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lurk not on every road to threaten (wayfarers), and to turn away from Allah's path him who believeth in Him, and to seek to make it crooked. And remember, when ye were but few, how He did multiply you. And see the nature of the consequence for the corrupters!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And do not lie in wait on every road to threaten and bar those who have faith in Him from the way of Allah, seeking to make it crooked. Remember when you were few, and He multiplied you, and observe how was the fate of the agents of corruption.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: 'Do not sit in every road, threatening and barring from the Path of Allah those who believed it, nor seek to make it crooked. Remember how He multiplied you when you were few in number. Consider the end of the corrupters.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And do not sit on every path, threatening and averting from the way of Allah those who believe in Him, seeking to make it [seem] deviant. And remember when you were few and He increased you. And see how was the end of the corrupters.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Do not ambush the believers or hinder them from every path that leads them to God just because you wish to make such ways seem crooked. Recall the time when you were just a few in number and God multiplied you. Consider how terrible the end of the evil-doers was!

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And do not lie in wait in every path, threatening and turning away from Allah's way him who believes in Him and seeking to make it crooked; and remember when you were few then He multiplied you, and consider what was the end of the mischief-makers.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Do not lie in ambush on every pathway, threatening people, barring those who believe from the Way of God, seeking to make it appear crooked. Remember when you were few in number and He multiplied you. Consider the fate of those who used to spread corruption.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "And squat not on every road, breathing threats, hindering from the path of Allah those who believe in Him, and seeking in it something crooked; But remember how ye were little, and He gave you increase. And hold in your mind's eye what was the end of those who did mischief.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wala taqAAudoo bikulli siratin tooAAidoona watasuddoona AAan sabeeli Allahi man amana bihi watabghoonaha AAiwajan waothkuroo ith kuntum qaleelan fakaththarakum waonthuroo kayfa kana AAaqibatu almufsideena

87.arabic: وَإِن كَانَ طَآئِفَةٌ مِّنكُمْ ءَامَنُوا۟ بِٱلَّذِىٓ أُرْسِلْتُ بِهِۦ وَطَآئِفَةٌ لَّمْ يُؤْمِنُوا۟ فَٱصْبِرُوا۟ حَتَّىٰ يَحْكُمَ ٱللَّهُ بَيْنَنَا ۚ وَهُوَ خَيْرُ ٱلْحَـٰكِمِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: If some of you believe what has been sent through me, and some of you do not, have patience until God decide between us, for He is the best of all judges."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “And if a group among you believes in what I have been sent with, and another group does not believe, then wait until Allah judges between us; and Allah’s judgement is the best of all.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And if there is a party of you who believe in the Message I have been sent with, and a party who believe not, be patient till God shall judge between us; He is the best of judges.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And if there be a party of you who believeth in that wherewith I am sent and a party who believeth not, then have patience until Allah judgeth between us, and He is the Best of judges.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "And if there is a party of you who believes in that with which I have been sent and a party who do not believe, so be patient until Allah judges between us, and He is the Best of judges."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “Since some of you believed in what I was sent with, and some did not believe, be patient until God judges between us; for He is the Best of Judges.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And if there are some among you who believe in the message that I bear while some do not believe, have patience till Allah shall judge between us. He is the best of those who judge.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "And if there is a party of you who believes in that with which I have been sent and a party who does not believe, so be patient until Allah judges between us, and He is the best of judges."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And if there is a party of you which believeth in that wherewith I have been sent, and there is a party which believeth not, then have patience until Allah judge between us. He is the Best of all who deal in judgment.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: If a group of you have believed in what I have been sent with, and a group have not believed, be patient until Allah judges between us, and He is the best of judges.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: If there are some among you who believe in that which I have been sent and others who disbelieve it, be patient until Allah shall judge between us. He is the best of judges'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And if there should be a group among you who has believed in that with which I have been sent and a group that has not believed, then be patient until Allah judges between us. And He is the best of judges."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: If there are some of you who believe in the Message that I have been commanded to preach and there are others who do not, exercise patience until God judges among us. He is the best Judge."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And if there is a party of you who believe in that with which am sent, and another party who do not believe, then wait patiently until Allah judges between us; and He is the best of the Judges.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: And if there is a group of you which believes in My message and others who disbelieve it, be patient until God shall judge between us. He is the best of judges."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "And if there is a party among you who believes in the message with which I have been sent, and a party which does not believe, hold yourselves in patience until Allah doth decide between us: for He is the best to decide.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wain kana taifatun minkum amanoo biallathee orsiltu bihi wataifatun lam yuminoo faisbiroo hatta yahkuma Allahu baynana wahuwa khayru alhakimeena

88.arabic: قَالَ ٱلْمَلَأُ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ مِن قَوْمِهِۦ لَنُخْرِجَنَّكَ يَـٰشُعَيْبُ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مَعَكَ مِن قَرْيَتِنَآ أَوْ لَتَعُودُنَّ فِى مِلَّتِنَا ۚ قَالَ أَوَلَوْ كُنَّا كَـٰرِهِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The arrogant leaders of the people replied: "We shall drive you away from our land, O Shu'aib, and those who are with you, unless you come back to your faith." But he remarked: "Even if we are disgusted with it?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: The proud leaders of his people said, “O Shuaib, we swear we will banish you and the Muslims who are with you, from our town or you must return to our religion”; he said, “Even though we detest it?”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Said the Council of those of his people who waxed proud, 'We will surely expel thee, O Shuaib, and those who believe with thee, from our city, unless you return into our creed.' He said, 'What, even though we detest it?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: The chiefs of those who were stiff-necked amongst his people said: surely we shall drive you forth, Shu'aib! and those who have believed with thee from our city, or else ye shall return unto our faith. He said: what! even though we be averse!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: The chiefs of those who were arrogant among his people said: "We shall certainly drive you out, O Shu'aib, and those who have believed with you from our town, or else you (all) shall return to our religion." He said: "Even though we hate it!

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: The arrogant elite among his people said, “O Shuaib, We will evict you from our town, along with those who believe with you, unless you return to our religion.” He said, “Even if we are unwilling?”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: The haughty elders of his people said: 'O Shu'ayb! We shall certainly banish you and your companions-in-faith from our town, or else you shall return to our faith.' Shu'ayb said: 'What! Even though we abhor [your faith]?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: The chiefs of those who were arrogant among his people said: "We shall certainly drive you out, O Shu`ayb and those who have believed with you from our town, or else you (all) shall return to our religion." He said: "Even though we hate it"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: The chieftains of his people, who were scornful, said: Surely we will drive thee out, O Shu'eyb, and those who believe with thee, from our township, unless ye return to our religion. He said: Even though we hate it?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: The elite of his people who were arrogant said, ‘O Shu'ayb, we will surely expel you and the faithful who are with you from our town, or else you shall revert to our creed.’ He said, ‘What! Even if we should be unwilling?!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: The proud assembly of his nation said: 'We will expel you Shu'aib and those who believe with you from our village unless you return to our creed' He replied: 'Even though we hate it?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Said the eminent ones who were arrogant among his people, "We will surely evict you, O Shu'ayb, and those who have believed with you from our city, or you must return to our religion." He said, "Even if we were unwilling?"

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: A proud group among Shu'ayb's people said, "We must expel you (Shu'ayb) and your followers from our town unless you give-up your faith and live as our own people." Shu'ayb asked them, "Will you use force against us?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: The chiefs, those who were proud from among his people said: We will most certainly turn you out, O Shu'aib, and (also; those who believe with you, from our town, or you shall come back to our faith. He said: What! though we dislike (it)?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: The arrogant leaders of his people said, "Shu'ayb, we will expel you and your fellow believers from our town unless you return to our faith." He said, "Even though we detest it?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: The leaders, the arrogant party among his people, said: "O Shu'aib! we shall certainly drive thee out of our city - (thee) and those who believe with thee; or else ye (thou and they) shall have to return to our ways and religion." He said: "What! even though we do detest (them)?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qala almalao allatheena istakbaroo min qawmihi lanukhrijannaka ya shuAAaybu waallatheena amanoo maAAaka min qaryatina aw lataAAoodunna fee millatina qala awalaw kunna kariheena

89.arabic: قَدِ ٱفْتَرَيْنَا عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبًا إِنْ عُدْنَا فِى مِلَّتِكُم بَعْدَ إِذْ نَجَّىٰنَا ٱللَّهُ مِنْهَا ۚ وَمَا يَكُونُ لَنَآ أَن نَّعُودَ فِيهَآ إِلَّآ أَن يَشَآءَ ٱللَّهُ رَبُّنَا ۚ وَسِعَ رَبُّنَا كُلَّ شَىْءٍ عِلْمًا ۚ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ تَوَكَّلْنَا ۚ رَبَّنَا ٱفْتَحْ بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَ قَوْمِنَا بِٱلْحَقِّ وَأَنتَ خَيْرُ ٱلْفَـٰتِحِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: We shall only be guilty of blaspheming God if we accept your way after the people of Noah, and gave you a greater increase in your stature. So think of the favours of God; you may haply be blessed."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “We shall then have fabricated a lie against Allah if we return to your religion after Allah has rescued us from it; and it is not for any of us Muslims to return to your religion except if Allah, Who is our Lord, wills; the knowledge of our Lord encompasses all things; in Allah only we have trusted; our Lord! Decide with justice between us and our people – and Yours is the best decision.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: We should have forged against God a lie if we returned into your creed; after God delivered us from it. It is not for us to return into it, unless God our Lord so will. Our Lord embraces all things in His knowledge. In God we have put our trust. Our Lord, give true deliverance between us and our people; Thou art the best of deliverers.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: We must have been fabricating a lie against Allah if we returned to your faith after Allah hath delivered us therefrom. And it is not for us to return thereunto except that Allah our Lord so willed; everything our Lord comprehendeth in His knowledge; in Allah we place our trust. O our Lord! decide Thou between us and our people with truth, Thou art the Best of the deciders.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "We should have invented a lie against Allah if we returned to your religion, after Allah has rescued us from it. And it is not for us to return to it unless Allah, our Lord, should will. Our Lord comprehends all things in His Knowledge. In Allah (Alone) we put our trust. Our Lord! Judge between us and our people in truth, for You are the Best of those who give judgment."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “We would be fabricating falsehood against God, if we were to return to your religion, after God has saved us from it. It is not for us to return to it, unless God, our Lord, wills. Our Lord embraces all things in knowledge. In God we place our trust. Our Lord, decide between us and our people in truth, for You are the Best of Deciders.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: If we return to your faith after Allah has delivered us from it we would be fabricating a lie against Allah. nor can we return to it again unless it be by, the will of Allah, our Lord. Our Lord has knowledge of all things, and in Allah we put our trust. Our Lord! Judge rightly between us and our people, for You are the best of those who judge.'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "We should have invented a lie against Allah if we returned to your religion, after Allah has rescued us from it. And it is not for us to return to it unless Allah, our Lord, should will. Our Lord comprehends all things in His knowledge. In Allah (Alone) we put our trust. Our Lord! Judge between us and our people in truth, for You are the best of those who give judgment."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: We should have invented a lie against Allah if we returned to your religion after Allah hath rescued us from it. It is not for us to return to it unless Allah our Lord should (so) will. Our Lord comprehendeth all things in knowledge. In Allah do we put our trust. Our Lord! Decide with truth between us and our folk, for Thou art the best of those who make decision.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: We would be fabricating a lie against Allah should we revert to your creed after Allah had delivered us from it. It does not behoove us to return to it, unless Allah, our Lord, should wish so. Our Lord embraces all things in [His] knowledge. In Allah we have put our trust.’ ‘Our Lord! Judge justly between us and our people, and You are the best of judges!’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We would have invented lies about Allah if we returned to your creed from which Allah has saved us, it is not for us that we return into it again except if Allah, our Lord wills. For our Lord has embraced everything with knowledge, and in Allah we have put our trust. Our Lord, open between us and our nation with truth, You are the Best of openers'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: We would have invented against Allah a lie if we returned to your religion after Allah had saved us from it. And it is not for us to return to it except that Allah, our Lord, should will. Our Lord has encompassed all things in knowledge. Upon Allah we have relied. Our Lord, decide between us and our people in truth, and You are the best of those who give decision."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We would certainly be inventing falsehoods against God if we were to accept your way of life, when God has already saved us from it. We do not have to accept it unless God, our Lord, wills it. Our Lord's knowledge covers all things. We trust in Him and ask Him to judge among us and our people, for He is the best Judge."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Indeed we shall have forged a lie against Allah If we go back to your religion after Allah has delivered us from It, and it befits us not that we should go back to it, except if Allah our Lord please: Our Lord comprehends all things in His knowledge; in Allah do we trust: Our Lord! decide between us and our people with truth; and Thou art the best of deciders.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We would be inventing lies against God if we returned to your faith after God has delivered us from it. It is not for us to return to it unless God our Lord so willed. Our Lord encompasses all things in His knowledge. We have put our trust in God. Our Lord, expose the truth [and judge] between us and our people, You are the best judge."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "We should indeed invent a lie against Allah, if we returned to your ways after Allah hath rescued us therefrom; nor could we by any manner of means return thereto unless it be as in the will and plan of Allah, Our Lord. Our Lord can reach out to the utmost recesses of things by His knowledge. In the Allah is our trust. our Lord! decide Thou between us and our people in truth, for Thou art the best to decide."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qadi iftarayna AAala Allahi kathiban in AAudna fee millatikum baAAda ith najjana Allahu minha wama yakoonu lana an naAAooda feeha illa an yashaa Allahu rabbuna wasiAAa rabbuna kulla shayin AAilman AAala Allahi tawakkalna rabbana iftah baynana wabayna qawmina bialhaqqi waanta khayru alfatiheena

90.arabic: وَقَالَ ٱلْمَلَأُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِن قَوْمِهِۦ لَئِنِ ٱتَّبَعْتُمْ شُعَيْبًا إِنَّكُمْ إِذًا لَّخَـٰسِرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The chiefs among his people who did not believe said (to them): "If you follow Shu'aib and his way you will surely be ruined."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And the disbelieving leaders of his people said, “If you obey Shuaib, you will indeed be in a loss.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Said the Council of those of his people who disbelieved, 'Now, if you follow Shuaib, assuredly in that case you will be losers.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And the chiefs of those who disbelieved amongst his people said: should ye follow Shu'aib, lo! verily ye will be the losers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: The chiefs of those who disbelieved among his people said (to their people): "If you follow Shu'aib, be sure then you will be the losers!"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: The elite of his people who disbelieved said, “If you follow Shuaib, you will be losers.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: The elders of his people who disbelieved said: 'Should you follow Shu'ayb, you will be utter losers.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: The chiefs of those who disbelieved among his people said (to their people): "If you follow Shu`ayb, be sure then you will be the losers!"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: But the chieftains of his people, who were disbelieving, said: If ye follow Shu'eyb, then truly ye shall be the losers.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: The elite of his people who were faithless said, ‘If you follow Shu'ayb, you will indeed be losers.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: But the unbelieving assembly of his nation said: 'If you follow Shu'aib, you shall assuredly be losers.'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Said the eminent ones who disbelieved among his people, "If you should follow Shu'ayb, indeed, you would then be losers."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: A group of the unbelievers among his people told the others, "If you follow Shu'ayb, you will certainly lose a great deal."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And the chiefs of those who disbelieved from among his people said: If you follow Shu'aib, you shall then most surely be losers

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: The leading men of his people who were bent on denying the truth, said, "If you follow Shu'ayb, you will certainly be the losers."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: The leaders, the unbelievers among his people, said: "If ye follow Shu'aib, be sure then ye are ruined!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waqala almalao allatheena kafaroo min qawmihi laini ittabaAAtum shuAAayban innakum ithan lakhasiroona

91.arabic: فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ ٱلرَّجْفَةُ فَأَصْبَحُوا۟ فِى دَارِهِمْ جَـٰثِمِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Then they were seized by an earthquake, and lay overturned on the ground in their homes in the morning.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Therefore the earthquake seized them – so at morning they remained lying flattened in their homes.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: So the earthquake seized them, and morning found them in their habitation fallen prostrate,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Whereupon an earthquake laid hold of them: so that they lay prone in their dwelling.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: So the earthquake seized them and they lay (dead), prostrate in their homes.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Thereupon, the quake struck them; and they became lifeless bodies in their homes.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Thereupon a shocking catastrophe seized them, and they remained prostrate in their dwellings.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: So the earthquake seized them and they lay (dead), prostrate in their homes.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: So the earthquake seized them and morning found them prostrate in their dwelling-place.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So the earthquake seized them, and they lay lifeless prostrate in their homes.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: So the earthquake seized them, at the morning they were crouched in their dwellings, dead.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So the earthquake seized them, and they became within their home [corpses] fallen prone.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Suddenly, an earthquake struck them and left them motionless in their homes.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Then the earthquake overtook them, so they became motionless bodies in their abode.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Thereupon an earthquake overtook them and morning found them lying flattened in their homes;

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: But the earthquake took them unawares, and they lay prostrate in their homes before the morning!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faakhathathumu alrrajfatu faasbahoo fee darihim jathimeena

92.arabic: ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ شُعَيْبًا كَأَن لَّمْ يَغْنَوْا۟ فِيهَا ۚ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ شُعَيْبًا كَانُوا۟ هُمُ ٱلْخَـٰسِرِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They who called Shu'aib a liar (disappeared) as though they had never existed; and those who called Shu'aib a liar were the ones who were ruined!

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: As if those who denied Shoaib had never lived in those homes; those who denied Shoaib, were themselves ruined.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: those who cried lies to Shuaib, as if never they dwelt there; those who cried lies to Shuaib, they were the losers.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Those who belied Shu'aib became as though they had never dwelt therein; those who belied Shu'aib, it is they who became the losers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Those who belied Shu'aib, became as if they had never dwelt there (in their homes). Those who belied Shu'aib, they were the losers.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those who rejected Shuaib—as if they never prospered therein. Those who rejected Shuaib—it was they who were the losers.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Those who had charged Shu'ayb with lying became as though-they had never lived there; it is they who became utter losers.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Those who belied Shu`ayb, became as if they had never dwelt there (in their homes). Those who belied Shu`ayb, they were the losers.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Those who denied Shu'eyb became as though they had not dwelt there. Those who denied Shu'eyb, they were the losers.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Those who impugned Shu'ayb became as if they had never lived there. Those who impugned Shu‘ayb were themselves the losers.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: It seemed as if those who had belied Shu'aib had never lived there. Those who belied Shu'aib were the losers.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Those who denied Shu'ayb - it was as though they had never resided there. Those who denied Shu'ayb - it was they who were the losers.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Those who called Shu'ayb a liar were destroyed as though they never existed. They certainly were the ones to lose a great deal.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Those who called Shu'aib a liar were as though they had never dwelt therein; those who called Shu'aib a liar, they were the losers.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: those who had denied Shu'ayb became as though they had never lived there. Those who denied Shu'ayb, were themselves the losers.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: The men who reject Shu'aib became as if they had never been in the homes where they had flourished: the men who rejected Shu'aib - it was they who were ruined!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Allatheena kaththaboo shuAAayban kaan lam yaghnaw feeha allatheena kaththaboo shuAAayban kanoo humu alkhasireena

93.arabic: فَتَوَلَّىٰ عَنْهُمْ وَقَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ لَقَدْ أَبْلَغْتُكُمْ رِسَـٰلَـٰتِ رَبِّى وَنَصَحْتُ لَكُمْ ۖ فَكَيْفَ ءَاسَىٰ عَلَىٰ قَوْمٍ كَـٰفِرِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: So Shu'aib turned away from them and said: "O people, I conveyed to you the message of my Lord, and warned you. (But you paid no heed). How can I grieve for a people who do not believe?"

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So Shoaib turned away from them saying, “O my people! Indeed I did deliver my Lord’s message to you and gave you sound advice; so why should I grieve for the disbelievers?” (The people in the graves can hear the speech of those who are on earth.)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: So he turned his back on them, and said, 'O my people, I have delivered to you the Messages of my Lord, and advised you sincerely; how should I grieve for a people of unbelievers?'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Then he turned from them, and said: my people! assuredly I delivered unto you the messages of my Lord, and counselled you good; how then should I lament over an unbelieving people?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then he (Shu'aib) turned from them and said: "O my people! I have indeed conveyed my Lord's Messages unto you and I have given you good advice. Then how can I sorrow for the disbelieving people's (destruction)."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: So he turned away from them, and said, “O my people, I have delivered to you the messages of my Lord, and I have advised you, so why should I grieve over a disbelieving people?”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Shu'ayb then departed from his people, and said: 'O my people! Surely I conveyed to you the message of my Lord, and gave you sincere advice. How, then, can I mourn for a people who refuse to accept the truth?'

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then he (Shu`ayb) turned from them and said: "O my people! I have indeed conveyed my Lord's Messages unto you and I have given you good advice. Then how can I grieve over people who are disbelievers."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: So he turned from them and said: O my people! I delivered my Lord's messages unto you and gave you good advice; then how can I sorrow for a people that rejected (truth)?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So he abandoned them [to their fate] and said, ‘O my people! Certainly, I communicated to you the messages of my Lord, and I was your well-wisher. So how should I grieve for a faithless lot?’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: He turned away from them saying: 'I conveyed to you, my nation, the messages of my Lord and advised you. How can I grieve for the unbelieving nation'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And he turned away from them and said, "O my people, I had certainly conveyed to you the messages of my Lord and advised you, so how could I grieve for a disbelieving people?"

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: He turned away from them saying, "My people, I preached the Message of my Lord to you and gave you good advice. How could I be sorry for the unbelievers?"

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: So he turned away from them and said: O my people! certainly I delivered to you the messages of my Lord and I gave you good advice; how shall I then be sorry for an unbelieving people?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: So he turned away from them, saying, "My people, I delivered my Lord's messages to you and gave you sincere advice, so why should I grieve for people who refused to believe?"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: So Shu'aib left them, saying: "O my people! I did indeed convey to you the messages for which I was sent by my Lord: I gave you good counsel, but how shall I lament over a people who refuse to believe!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fatawalla AAanhum waqala ya qawmi laqad ablaghtukum risalati rabbee wanasahtu lakum fakayfa asa AAala qawmin kafireena

94.arabic: وَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَا فِى قَرْيَةٍ مِّن نَّبِىٍّ إِلَّآ أَخَذْنَآ أَهْلَهَا بِٱلْبَأْسَآءِ وَٱلضَّرَّآءِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَضَّرَّعُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: There is not a region to which We sent a prophet and did not inflict upon its people adversity and hardship so that they may submit.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And never did We send any Prophet to a dwelling but We seized its people with hardship and adversity so that they may become humble.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: We have sent no Prophet to any city but that We seized its people with misery and hardship, that haply they might be humble;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And We sent not a prophet to any township but We laid hold of the people thereof with tribulation and distress that haply they may humble themselves.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And We sent no Prophet unto any town (and they denied him), but We seized its people with suffering from extreme poverty (or loss in wealth) and loss of health and calamities, so that they might humiliate themselves (and repent to Allah).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: We did not send any prophet to any town but We afflicted its people with misery and adversity, so that they may humble themselves.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Never have We sent a Prophet to a place without trying its people with adversity and hardship that they may humble themselves.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And We sent no Prophet unto any town (and they denied him), but We seized its people with Ba'sa' and Darra', so that they might humble themselves (to Allah).

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And We sent no prophet unto any township but We did afflict its folk with tribulation and adversity that haply they might grow humble.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: We did not send a prophet to any town without visiting its people with stress and distress so that they might entreat [for Allah’s forgiveness].

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We sent no Prophet to a village but seized its inhabitants with misfortune and adversity in order that they might be humble in supplication.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And We sent to no city a prophet [who was denied] except that We seized its people with poverty and hardship that they might humble themselves [to Allah].

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: To every town that We sent a Prophet, We tested its inhabitants through distress and adversity so that perhaps they would submit themselves to Us.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And We did not send a prophet in a town but We overtook its people with distress and affliction in order that they might humble themselves.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Whenever We sent a prophet to a town, We afflicted its people with suffering and adversity, so that they might humble themselves [before God],

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Whenever We sent a prophet to a town, We took up its people in suffering and adversity, in order that they might learn humility.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wama arsalna fee qaryatin min nabiyyin illa akhathna ahlaha bialbasai waalddarrai laAAallahum yaddarraAAoona

95.arabic: ثُمَّ بَدَّلْنَا مَكَانَ ٱلسَّيِّئَةِ ٱلْحَسَنَةَ حَتَّىٰ عَفَوا۟ وَّقَالُوا۟ قَدْ مَسَّ ءَابَآءَنَا ٱلضَّرَّآءُ وَٱلسَّرَّآءُ فَأَخَذْنَـٰهُم بَغْتَةً وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But when We changed hardship to ease, and they rose and prospered, (they forgot Our favours) and said: "Our ancestors had also known suffering and joy." So We caught them unawares.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Then We changed the misfortune into prosperity to the extent that they became numerous and said, “Indeed grief and comfort did reach our ancestors” – so We seized them suddenly in their neglect.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: then We gave them in the place of evil good, till they multiplied, and said, 'Hardship and happiness visited our fathers.' So We seized them suddenly, unawares.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Thereafter We substituted ease in place of adversity until they abounded and said: even Thus tribulation and prosperity touched our fathers. Then We laid hold of them of a sudden, while they perceived not.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then We changed the evil for the good, until they increased in number and in wealth, and said: "Our fathers were touched with evil (loss of health and calamities) and with good (prosperity, etc.)." So We seized them of a sudden while they were unaware.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Then We substituted prosperity in place of hardship. Until they increased in number, and said, “Adversity and prosperity has touched our ancestors.” Then We seized them suddenly, while they were unaware.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Then We changed adversity into ease until they throve and said: 'Our forefathers had also seen both adversity and prosperity.' So We suddenly seized them without their even perceiving it.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then We changed the evil for the good, until they `Afaw (increased), and said: "Our fathers were touched with evil and with good." So We seized them all of a sudden while they were unaware.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Then changed We the evil plight for good till they grew affluent and said: Tribulation and distress did touch our fathers. Then We seized them unawares, when they perceived not.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Then We changed the ill [conditions] to good until they multiplied [in numbers] and said, ‘Adversity and ease befell our fathers [too].’ Then We seized them suddenly while they were unaware.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Then We changed evil for good, until they multiplied and said: 'Our fathers were indeed touched by misfortune and adversity' So We seized them suddenly while they were unaware.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Then We exchanged in place of the bad [condition], good, until they increased [and prospered] and said, "Our fathers [also] were touched with hardship and ease." So We seized them suddenly while they did not perceive.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We then replaced their misfortune with well-being until they were relieved and began saying, "Our fathers had also experienced good and bad days." Suddenly, We struck them (with torment) while they were all unaware (of what was happening).

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Then We gave them good in the place of evil until they became many and said: Distress and happiness did indeed befall our fathers. Then We took them by surprise while they did not perceive.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and then We changed their hardship into ease until they grew affluent and said, "Our fathers had also experienced adversity and prosperity," then We seized them suddenly, unawares.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Then We changed their suffering into prosperity, until they grew and multiplied, and began to say: "Our fathers (too) were touched by suffering and affluence"... Behold! We called them to account of a sudden, while they realised not (their peril).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Thumma baddalna makana alssayyiati alhasanata hatta AAafaw waqaloo qad massa abaana alddarrao waalssarrao faakhathnahum baghtatan wahum la yashAAuroona

96.arabic: وَلَوْ أَنَّ أَهْلَ ٱلْقُرَىٰٓ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَٱتَّقَوْا۟ لَفَتَحْنَا عَلَيْهِم بَرَكَـٰتٍ مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَـٰكِن كَذَّبُوا۟ فَأَخَذْنَـٰهُم بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَكْسِبُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But if the people of these regions had believed and feared God, We would surely have showered on them blessings of the heavens and the earth; but they only denied, and We punished them for their deeds.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And had the people of the dwellings believed and been pious, We would have surely opened for them the blessings from the sky and from the earth, but in fact they denied, and We therefore seized them on account of their deeds.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Yet had the peoples of the cities believed and been godfearing, We would have opened upon them blessings from heaven and earth; but they cried lies, and so We seized them for what they earned.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And had the people of those townships believed and feared, We would of a surety have opened up to them blessings from the heavens and the earth; but they belied, wherefore We laid hold of them for that which they had been earning.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And if the people of the towns had believed and had the Taqwa (piety), certainly, We should have opened for them blessings from the heaven and the earth, but they belied (the Messengers). So We took them (with punishment) for what they used to earn (polytheism and crimes, etc.).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Had the people of the towns believed and turned righteous, We would have opened for them the blessings of the heaven and the earth; but they rejected the truth, so We seized them by what they were doing.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Had the people of those towns believed and been God-fearing, We would certainly have opened up to them blessings from the heavens and the earth; but they gave the lie [to their Prophets] and so We seized them for their deeds. so.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And if the people of the towns had believed and had Taqwa, certainly, We should have opened for them blessings from the heaven and the earth, but they belied (the Messengers). So We took them (with punishment) for what they used to earn.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And if the people of the townships had believed and kept from evil, surely We should have opened for them blessings from the sky and from the earth. But (unto every messenger) they gave the lie, and so We seized them on account of what they used to earn.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: If the people of the towns had been faithful and Godwary, We would have opened to them blessings from the heaven and the earth. But they denied; so We seized them because of what they used to earn.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Had the people of the villages believed and been cautious, We would have opened upon them the blessings from heaven and earth. But they belied, and We seized them for what they earned.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And if only the people of the cities had believed and feared Allah, We would have opened upon them blessings from the heaven and the earth; but they denied [the messengers], so We seized them for what they were earning."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Had the people of the towns believed (in Our revelations) and maintained piety, We would have certainly showered on them Our blessings from the sky and the earth. But they called Our revelations lies, thus Our torment struck them for their evil deeds.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And if the people of the towns had believed and guarded (against evil) We would certainly have opened up for them blessings from the heaven and the earth, but they rejected, so We overtook them for what they had earned.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Had the people of those towns believed in and feared God, We would have showered upon them blessings from heaven and earth, but they rejected the truth. So We seized them on account of their misdeeds.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: If the people of the towns had but believed and feared Allah, We should indeed have opened out to them (All kinds of) blessings from heaven and earth; but they rejected (the truth), and We brought them to book for their misdeeds.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walaw anna ahla alqura amanoo waittaqaw lafatahna AAalayhim barakatin mina alssamai waalardi walakin kaththaboo faakhathnahum bima kanoo yaksiboona

97.arabic: أَفَأَمِنَ أَهْلُ ٱلْقُرَىٰٓ أَن يَأْتِيَهُم بَأْسُنَا بَيَـٰتًا وَهُمْ نَآئِمُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Are the people of the region so secure that they lie asleep unaware as Our punishment overtakes them?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Do the people of the dwellings not fear that Our wrath may come upon them at night while they are asleep?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Do the people of the cities feel secure Our might shall not come upon them at night while they are sleeping?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Are the people of the townships then secure that Our wrath would not come upon them at night while they are slumbering?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Did the people of the towns then feel secure against the coming of Our Punishment by night while they are asleep?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Do the people of the towns feel secure that Our might will not come upon them by night, while they sleep?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Do the people of those towns feel secure that Our punishment will not come to them at night while they are asleep?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Did the people of the towns then feel secure against the coming of Our punishment by night while they were asleep

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Are the people of the townships then secure from the coming of Our wrath upon them as a night-raid while they sleep?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Do the people of the towns feel secure from Our punishment overtaking them at night while they are asleep?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Do the inhabitants of the villages feel secure from Our Might coming to them at night whilst they sleep?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Then, did the people of the cities feel secure from Our punishment coming to them at night while they were asleep?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Did the people of the towns think themselves secure from Our wrath that could strike them at night during their sleep

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: What! do the people of the towns then feel secure from Our punishment coming to them by night while they sleep?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Do the people of these towns now feel secure against the coming of Our punishment upon them by night while they are asleep?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Did the people of the towns feel secure against the coming of Our wrath by night while they were asleep?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Afaamina ahlu alqura an yatiyahum basuna bayatan wahum naimoona

98.arabic: أَوَأَمِنَ أَهْلُ ٱلْقُرَىٰٓ أَن يَأْتِيَهُم بَأْسُنَا ضُحًى وَهُمْ يَلْعَبُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Or, are the people of the towns so unafraid that (even when) Our retribution comes upon them in the hours of the morning they remain engrossed in sport and play?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Or do the people of the dwellings not fear that Our wrath may come upon them during the day, while they are playing?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Do the people of the cities feel secure Our might shall not come upon them in daylight while they are playing?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Or, are the people of the township secure that Our wrath would not come upon them by daylight while they are disporting themselves?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Or, did the people of the towns then feel secure against the coming of Our Punishment in the forenoon while they play?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Do the people of the towns feel secure that Our might will not come upon them by day, while they play?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Or, do the people of those towns feel secure that Our punishment will not come to them by daylight while they are at play?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Or, did the people of the towns then feel secure against the coming of Our punishment in the forenoon while they were playing

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Or are the people of the townships then secure from the coming of Our wrath upon them in the daytime while they play?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Do the people of the towns feel secure from Our punishment overtaking them at midday while they are playing around?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Or do the inhabitants of the villages feel secure from Our Might coming to them in the midmorning while they play?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Or did the people of the cities feel secure from Our punishment coming to them in the morning while they were at play?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: or that which could seize them during their busy hours of the day?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: What! do the people of the towns feel secure from Our punishment coming to them in the morning while they play?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Or, do they feel secure against the coming of Our punishment upon them in the forenoon while they are at play?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Or else did they feel secure against its coming in broad daylight while they played about (care-free)?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Awa amina ahlu alqura an yatiyahum basuna duhan wahum yalAAaboona

99.arabic: أَفَأَمِنُوا۟ مَكْرَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ فَلَا يَأْمَنُ مَكْرَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَّا ٱلْقَوْمُ ٱلْخَـٰسِرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Can they remain secure against the plan of God? Only they feel secure against the plan of God who are certain of being ruined.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Are they oblivious to Allah’s secret plan? So none is unafraid of Allah’s secret plan except the people of ruin!

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Do they feel secure against God's devising? None feels secure against God's devising but the people of the lost.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Are then they secure against the contrivance of Allah? And none feeleth secure against the contrivance of Allah except the people who are losers.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Did they then feel secure against the Plan of Allah. None feels secure from the Plan of Allah except the people who are the losers.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Do they feel safe from God’s plan? None feel safe from God’s plan except the losing people.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Do they feel secure against the design of Allah None can feel secure against the design of Allah except the utter losers.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Did they then feel secure against Allah's plan None feels secure from Allah's plan except the people who are the losers.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Are they then secure from Allah's scheme? None deemeth himself secure from Allah's scheme save folk that perish.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Do they feel secure from Allah’s devising? No one feels secure from Allah’s devising except the people who are losers.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: (In reply to your devising) do they feel secure from the devising of Allah? None feels secure from the devising of Allah except the loosing nation.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Then did they feel secure from the plan of Allah? But no one feels secure from the plan of Allah except the losing people.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Did they consider themselves secure from the retribution of God? No one can have such attitude except those who are lost.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: What! do they then feel secure from Allah's plan? But none feels secure from Allah's plan except the people who shall perish.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Do they feel secure against God's devising? No one feels secure against God's devising except for those doomed to ruin.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Did they then feel secure against the plan of Allah?- but no one can feel secure from the Plan of Allah, except those (doomed) to ruin!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Afaaminoo makra Allahi fala yamanu makra Allahi illa alqawmu alkhasiroona

100.arabic: أَوَلَمْ يَهْدِ لِلَّذِينَ يَرِثُونَ ٱلْأَرْضَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ أَهْلِهَآ أَن لَّوْ نَشَآءُ أَصَبْنَـٰهُم بِذُنُوبِهِمْ ۚ وَنَطْبَعُ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ فَهُمْ لَا يَسْمَعُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Do not the people who inherited the earth from the (earlier) inhabitants perceive that We could afflict them too for their sins if We pleased, and put seals on their hearts that they may not hear (the voice of truth)?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Or did not those who inherited the land after its owners, get enough guidance that if We will, We can afflict them with calamity for their sins? And We set seals upon their hearts so they do not hear.

translations.author: Arberry
